body: ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Peter, Now that it’s officially 2025, it’s time to finalize our priorities for the year! https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=b40c5afc0a9fac02315fcdd9f51e2dd4cddca3cf0db0413f5ca4c653840b3a3989b334239ba43c11d8b79944f05c28991928e89409148bc74e1fde94561d0ad6 COMPLETE MY 2025 PRIORITIES SURVEY One of my top priorities this year is staying connected with the most dedicated members of my grassroots team, including YOU! Your voice and perspective are incredibly important to our conservative movement. With Donald Trump set to be sworn in as the 47th President of the United States and a new Republican Majority in the Senate, this is a pivotal moment for America! There will be many challenges ahead as we work to repair our country after four years of the failed Biden-Harris Administration, but I know that together we can rise to meet them. https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=b40c5afc0a9fac02315fcdd9f51e2dd4cddca3cf0db0413f5ca4c653840b3a3989b334239ba43c11d8b79944f05c28991928e89409148bc74e1fde94561d0ad6 But this is why it’s so crucial to finalize our priorities and agenda for the year ASAP! I’m reaching out to you today because I value your input. As a key member of Team Marsha, your thoughts on what should be prioritized in the coming year are important to me. We can’t afford to leave anything to chance, and your feedback will ensure Republicans are focused on what matters most. https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=b40c5afc0a9fac02315fcdd9f51e2dd4cddca3cf0db0413f5ca4c653840b3a3989b334239ba43c11d8b79944f05c28991928e89409148bc74e1fde94561d0ad6 If you have a few minutes to spare, I’d greatly appreciate it if you could take my 2025 Priorities Survey before the day ends. https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=b40c5afc0a9fac02315fcdd9f51e2dd4cddca3cf0db0413f5ca4c653840b3a3989b334239ba43c11d8b79944f05c28991928e89409148bc74e1fde94561d0ad6 COMPLETE MY 2025 PRIORITIES SURVEY It’s brief, but your response will give me a clear picture of what’s important to you and where you think our efforts should be directed in 2025. Thank You and Happy New Year, Marsha Blackburn Paid for by Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Marsha for Senate, MARSHA PAC, and Tennessee Republican Party Federal Election Account. name: Hillsdale College via Reclaiming American Freedom
subject: Who rules whom?
body:
The Constitution of "We the People" is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.

This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | |
---
| | [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=4173cb4c80cf4122a35a6a9578a69f38&_e=Ub7Y0uv-zKWGewf5QH5jRVB3ZFxxYGcMzJLIHMK8EzxBeawG8PqLEazgKWjXsepRTGJP_7CMKTRY-_BUHHowEUuabP7PO_d5t_LH7TJZUgoJr-teKt6O6N1lJdxHbUK9hl_PHWzFzvb2ID5FZG1Z8isvTXCcXWuBjuteRnSzz_Yq9gE_AvodASib2e099oUvj-vq0zJXsWZyjy-sJPdNTBj7EkLPwSoCybVvtHYoQ31WRtuC3d05Tildz074rhMJgTMfaJrJcfpou_4l0gcmJOxsMAbBVKtbACkTZSJsYatJ4Md1ACytvPB_AkQkiQ6lDRPdjL3bAdfU097Q99eC2cU5cgNY1h9TjGd4ZTofSP88Qu5t5eD6RSHZsYTgFB0pkxtJEH2a_kVKKhHNxm0w5nZNs7yTA0Wun4MsimfMaB8%3D)
---
| | | Dear Fellow American, Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its [**National Referendum on the Administrative State**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=4173cb4c80cf4122a35a6a9578a69f38&_e=Ub7Y0uv-zKWGewf5QH5jRVB3ZFxxYGcMzJLIHMK8EzxBeawG8PqLEazgKWjXsepRTGJP_7CMKTRY-_BUHHowEUuabP7PO_d5t_LH7TJZUgoJr-teKt6O6N1lJdxHbUK9hl_PHWzFzvb2ID5FZG1Z8isvTXCcXWuBjuteRnSzz_Yq9gE_AvodASib2e099oUvj-vq0zJXsWZyjy-sJPdNTBj7EkLPwSoCybVvtHYoQ31WRtuC3d05Tildz074rhMJdPceSZqezt2EFQP4DjVvBc74rwi8g0agY3McW5El0mIEKYeQTdXk6R5aHjsEnKgm06eIRvPhB-R1rebBTw5jmZG6ESfbHSalAAKxML3JEZ7tse_Us65U8uwmnP1aNLnflT2nr-zm7aWlpKDSNQck04cDECbECh39SghjjngIrw8%3D).

Responding will only take a few minutes. Your input as an informed patriot is needed right away...because **our country is facing a constitutional crisis**.

It's a crisis that involves a fundamental political question: **who rules whom?**

The Constitution established a limited government in which the **people** ruled through their elected officials. Under this form of government, America became the freest and most prosperous nation in history.

Today, however, our constitutional system is one that increasingly exists in name only. **The Constitution of "We the People" is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.** This means we live in an era of growing bureaucratic despotism, manifested in an administrative state that bypasses the Constitution, rejects the separation of powers, and replaces the rule of law with regulations.

**But we can 't just TALK about the problem. We also have to TAKE ACTION--to DO SOMETHING about it, while there's still time to save our country. **

That is why I'm writing to you today.

Hillsdale College is committed to returning to the Constitution of "We the People"--before it's too late.

To do that, I need your input. What are your opinions on this topic--knowing that we can propose SOLUTIONS that will restore liberty?

**Please let me know your thoughts today by answering the National Referendum on the Administrative State using this secure link:**

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=4173cb4c80cf4122a35a6a9578a69f38&_e=Ub7Y0uv-zKWGewf5QH5jRVB3ZFxxYGcMzJLIHMK8EzxBeawG8PqLEazgKWjXsepRTGJP_7CMKTRY-_BUHHowEUuabP7PO_d5t_LH7TJZUgoJr-teKt6O6N1lJdxHbUK9hl_PHWzFzvb2ID5FZG1Z8isvTXCcXWuBjuteRnSzz_Yq9gE_AvodASib2e099oUvj-vq0zJXsWZyjy-sJPdNTBj7EkLPwSoCybVvtHYoQ31WRtuC3d05Tildz074rhMJvjj77r3yEzaeMLvfpsx9VcegGztYSPyzGeMyxcpLxI47mLGsRftJphyOu_nrI_3d8-QTNlgKus_-ljV9ylSWcLp_elWET_-iJ-2PlA7wZn12DJpAQkBMjps-IHcpitOvTMHlwc6z0suywm-IjRGsXjStKMbTflvUQXJ5F--AfpI%3D)**

When you send in your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

The rise of the administrative state is a grave threat to our country.

To counter this threat, Hillsdale sponsors several educational outreach efforts--including _Imprimis_ , free online courses, programs on our Washington, D.C., campus, and a growing network of classical K-12 schools and homeschoolers--to teach citizens about the meaning of the Constitution and the importance of our founding principles of liberty.

Your gift of at least $15--or even $25, $35, $50, $100, or more if you're especially motivated to TAKE ACTION--will help us to expand these educational initiatives that are reaching and teaching millions.

Your support of these efforts is essential because Hillsdale refuses to take ONE PENNY of government funding.

So please complete your **National Referendum on the Administrative State** and consider giving your most generous tax-deductible contribution.

Thank you for being on the side of the Constitution and liberty!

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

**P.S.** I look forward to receiving your opinions about what's happening in our nation. And I hope that you can support our work.

So please complete the **National Referendum on the Administrative State** using this secure link:

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=4173cb4c80cf4122a35a6a9578a69f38&_e=Ub7Y0uv-zKWGewf5QH5jRVB3ZFxxYGcMzJLIHMK8EzxBeawG8PqLEazgKWjXsepRTGJP_7CMKTRY-_BUHHowEUuabP7PO_d5t_LH7TJZUgoJr-teKt6O6N1lJdxHbUK9hl_PHWzFzvb2ID5FZG1Z8isvTXCcXWuBjuteRnSzz_Yq9gE_AvodASib2e099oUvj-vq0zJXsWZyjy-sJPdNTBj7EkLPwSoCybVvtHYoQ31WRtuC3d05Tildz074rhMJGMPy0NNW8BWQ0ERE0dLmENMva_EQXUYHtO3LhHj1VojYb-CpkJdYl_6HIvt6oSut3t1_cI27mjWNM_aPhWiUeVbgAu3FZSHi_fIdTO9Cb3CUvaGv8WIEJdARyERlZCHVuHmG1OJIfB2kPosNWA44Cqib0yJ5rgJUjrKiyTopk80%3D)**

As soon as you respond, we will begin your FREE lifetime subscription to _Imprimis_ , Hillsdale's digest of liberty that you will receive via email ten times a year. If you'd like, you can also sign up to receive the publication through postal mail.

Educating millions about the Constitution to counter the threat from the administrative state is a monumental task. That's why when you complete your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Thank you for your time and attention--and for your commitment to liberty.
---
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

Reclaiming American Freedom
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
[**unsubscribe**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_s=4173cb4c80cf4122a35a6a9578a69f38&_t=lteeKAtfBHPtnSXQrNrXxCpGwKvyRW1jDwek7lgcSCVXbQ9bcdoIFo30-czGv0Vd3olq8fA7GI3Gm6JTAAWak129lpVrAb1hOMGr5LHvkm8AVD9Bykn3Z2ZaEGfAp4AH)

name: Grover Norquist
subject: a personal favor
body:
We are at a pivotal moment in our nation's history.

*|MC_PREVIEW_TEXT|* |
---
| | [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=d08c24c2bcf048109e2cfd8ec5c2f816&_e=29ULFiWx-4YHV-afJMldw2iCkkAoQcyMXUdeVlrNdfB85Xt0ec_Rk78uwPIKmV43-e71Td9YGPfbZSI1LSiUJsywtjskFqVbGCRfRWoX0vvNY2Yh55DD30-LEkwXZkj3uYWid-i1lyZWQ5jeCWKYVXbJHdmjt8SkcX9XShtCpyWX0TZV8fUlaYKfIWc3e1xu_7pdIEHHFrL1T9zSmzqIkmHrZLTky4N06S80tLZ7Q_PKFbyyMiFdJ44n_TDrhUbp76dCSLDNHtK6ppAxWjGBa_9m9BGrtG2LA-mT-qqDb5o%3D)
Friend,

We are at a pivotal moment in our nation's history. The values that have made America a beacon of freedom and prosperity are under relentless attack.

As a steadfast advocate for limited government and individual liberty, I have had the honor of working alongside some of the nation's most dedicated and principled organizations.

**Today, I am reaching out to you on behalf of one such organization that has consistently championed the values we hold dear: the Goldwater Institute.**

The Goldwater Institute is on the front lines of this battle, scoring real wins for freedom in state courts, legislatures, and communities nationwide. But they cannot do it alone.

[I am personally helping the Goldwater Institute raise $50,000 this month. Your support is critical for us to continue our success in scoring real wins for freedom from coast to coast and empowering all Americans to live freer, happier lives. Can you join me by donating today? The values that have made America a beacon of freedom and prosperity are under relentless attack. As a steadfast advocate for limited government and individual liberty, I have had the honor of working alongside some of the nation's most dedicated and principled organizations. Today, I am reaching out to you on behalf of one such organization that has consistently championed the values we hold dear: the Goldwater Institute. The Goldwater Institute is on the front lines of this battle, scoring real wins for freedom in state courts, legislatures, and communities nationwide. But they cannot do it alone. The Goldwater Institute is at the forefront of the fight to defend America’s founding values, crafting and promoting policy solutions that empower individuals, reduce government overreach, and safeguard our constitutional rights. From healthcare reform to educational choice, the Goldwater Institute's impact resonates across Arizona and the entire country. As a board member, I have witnessed firsthand the incredible work the Goldwater Institute does. Their commitment to free-market principles and limited government is unwavering. The Goldwater Institute is not just talking about change; they are making it happen. But to continue their critical work, they need the support of dedicated patriots like you. Your contribution to the Goldwater Institute will help: 1. Defend Individual Liberties: By challenging unconstitutional laws that infringe on our rights. 2. Promote Economic Freedom: Through policies that reduce the tax burden, eliminate unnecessary regulations, and foster a thriving business environment. 3. Expand School Choice: Ensuring that every child has access to the best possible education, regardless of their zip code or economic status. 4. Protect Healthcare Freedom: Advocating for patient-centered reforms that put control back into the hands of individuals and families. Thank you for your commitment to our shared principles. name: Ted Cruz
subject: Happy New Year!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
We’re ramping up to get big things done in 2025. Read more >>>

| | |
---
| | Fellow Conservative, It's official now - 2024 is a wrap and the New Year is here.

I want to wish you and your family good health, happiness, and prosperity as we celebrate this season of renewal today!
---
---
November 5, 2024 was a turning point for our great nation. Voters rejected the extreme woke-left agenda that the Democrats have embraced and we won a clean sweep of the White House, control of the Senate, and control of the House.

2025 is our new beginning to SAVE AMERICA.

We're hard at work ramping up our efforts to get big things done this year. Thank you so much for being part of our fight-to-win grassroots team.

Happy New Year and God Bless America!

\- Ted

name: Grover Norquist
subject: a personal favor
body:
We are at a pivotal moment in our nation's history.

*|MC_PREVIEW_TEXT|* |
---
| | [](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyU0L2K6zAUBOCnkTsb_dmyCxUXgrvcdktxbJ11BLIkpOPk9ZcE9gG2nmH4mK-7u0OIIR3_4URLlVxtLhdyR_QvoNofNT-x9t_wdJLLseei58LJzlttpFdTh1YYNRrNZ8O7hx2VFMsiAEY-b0KjMjDraUK9jH7BTXTBvne44EIoMY18gHkz4MF45XH32jPNIWKlNtADK5Zri2GHRAjnsOezi_ZBVBpT_5hcmVwb7lfFARL6TO8Gk-uRP3ysfUiNAl2ETK4Fetgp5MTUetHpdjgLhCMxdfs1MTl9kpwIEzF1o0pdtb68QoyhTYZpfpwQ4odCf3nsaeVPAAAA___BUncS)
Friend,

We are at a pivotal moment in our nation's history. The values that have made America a beacon of freedom and prosperity are under relentless attack.

As a steadfast advocate for limited government and individual liberty, I have had the honor of working alongside some of the nation's most dedicated and principled organizations.

**Today, I am reaching out to you on behalf of one such organization that has consistently championed the values we hold dear: the Goldwater Institute.**

The Goldwater Institute is on the front lines of this battle, scoring real wins for freedom in state courts, legislatures, and communities nationwide. But they cannot do it alone.

[I am personally helping the Goldwater Institute raise $50,000 this month. Your support is critical for us to continue our success in scoring real wins for freedom from coast to coast and empowering all Americans to live freer, happier lives. Can you join me by donating today? Click here to donate >>](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyU0L2K6zAUBOCnkTsb_dmyCxUXgrvcdktxbJ11BLIkpOPk9ZcE9gG2nmH4mK-7u0OIIR3_4URLlVxtLhdyR_QvoNofNT-x9t_wdJLLseei58LJzlttpFdTh1YYNRrNZ8O7hx2VFMsiAEY-b0KjMjDraUK9jH7BTXTBvne44EIoMY18gHkz4MF45XH32jPNIWKlNtADK5Zri2GHRAjnsOezi_ZBVBpT_5hcmVwb7lfFARL6TO8Gk-uRP3ysfUiNAl2ETK4Fetgp5MTUetHpdjgLhCMxdfs1MTl9kpwIEzF1o0pdtb68QoyhTYZpfpwQ4odCf3nsaeVPAAAA___BUncS)

The Goldwater Institute is at the forefront of the fight to defend America’s founding values, crafting and promoting policy solutions that empower individuals, reduce government overreach, and safeguard our constitutional rights.

From healthcare reform to educational choice, the Goldwater Institute's impact resonates across Arizona and the entire country.

As a board member, I have witnessed firsthand the incredible work the Goldwater Institute does. Their commitment to free-market principles and limited government is unwavering. **_The Goldwater Institute is not just talking about change; they are making it happen_.**

But to continue their critical work, they need the support of dedicated patriots like you. Your contribution to the Goldwater Institute will help:
1. **Defend Individual Liberties:** By challenging unconstitutional laws that infringe on our rights.
2. **Promote Economic Freedom:** Through policies that reduce the tax burden, eliminate unnecessary regulations, and foster a thriving business environment.
3. **Expand School Choice:** Ensuring that every child has access to the best possible education, regardless of their zip code or economic status.
4. **Protect Healthcare Freedom:** Advocating for patient-centered reforms that put control back into the hands of individuals and families.

[I urge you to join me in supporting the Goldwater Institute by making a generous donation today. Your investment in this vital work will help secure a future where freedom, opportunity, and prosperity flourish for all Americans.](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyU0L2K6zAUBOCnkTsb_dmyCxUXgrvcdktxbJ11BLIkpOPk9ZcE9gG2nmH4mK-7u0OIIR3_4URLlVxtLhdyR_QvoNofNT-x9t_wdJLLseei58LJzlttpFdTh1YYNRrNZ8O7hx2VFMsiAEY-b0KjMjDraUK9jH7BTXTBvne44EIoMY18gHkz4MF45XH32jPNIWKlNtADK5Zri2GHRAjnsOezi_ZBVBpT_5hcmVwb7lfFARL6TO8Gk-uRP3ysfUiNAl2ETK4Fetgp5MTUetHpdjgLhCMxdfs1MTl9kpwIEzF1o0pdtb68QoyhTYZpfpwQ4odCf3nsaeVPAAAA___BUncS)

name: Douglas Carswell
subject: Mississippi is now one of the fastest growing states. Here’s a Blueprint to go further.
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/msblueprint?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| Dear Friend, **Did you know that Mississippi is now one of the fastest growing states in America?** Only two states saw real GDP rise faster than it did here in the third quarter of 2024.

Were you aware that personal income in our state rose more here than almost anywhere in the US this past year?

New data from the Bureau of Economic Analysis shows that Mississippi is on the up.
---
|
---
_Getting richer, faster: Mississippi income growth is one of the fastest in America_
| **For as long as anyone can remember, Mississippi has ranked 50 th out of 50. Not for much longer, perhaps.** According to this new data, ours’s was one of the top performing states in 2024. If we keep growing for the next few years the way we did in 2024, we won’t be bottom of the class for much longer.

**Mississippi’s success is not an accident. It’s a consequence of a number of key free market reforms:**

* Labor market deregulation, with an Occupational Licensing law in 2021.
* Tax cuts with legislation to cut the state income tax to a flat 4 percent in 2022.
* Further tax reform to make it more tax efficient for businesses in 2023.
* Education funding reform as a step towards school choice in 2024.

**These reforms have begun to energize our state.** They make it easier for people to get ahead, for businesses to invest, and for families to spend their income on their priorities. They draw in inward investment, which is changing our state for the better.
---
|
---
_Powering ahead: Mississippi output growth is one the fastest in America_
| **If Mississippi is not to lose this momentum, we need to go even further.** That is why MCPP has just published a _Blueprint for Mississippi _– a list of the ten key reforms that would lift our state to the top of the economic table.

**The number one reform we need to prosper is school choice.** Why? School choice is the only way to be certain of raising standards. The better job we do of educating young people, the greater their chances of leading a prosperous, fulfilling life.

Our _Blueprint_ sets out how we can accomplish school choice, giving every family in our state the choices that today only the very rich enjoy.

**To prosper, our state needs less regulation and less government.** Our _Blueprint _sets out proposals to cut taxes further and dismantle the costly, leftist bureaucracy that seems to be in control no matter who you vote for.

**Decades of crony cartel politics has stifled innovation in our state. **Years of lobbyists cutting cozy deals in the Capitol that commercially advantage their clients has held Mississippi back. A lot of the intentionally restrictive laws that limit health care provision simply need to go. Our _Blueprint_ sets out how to make this happen.

MCPP has been a driving force behind many of the key free market reforms that have helped energize our state. But at every opportunity, crony cartel politics has tried to prevent change.

The crony cartel will try again. It’s what self-serving cartels do. Already they are mobilizing half-baked arguments against school choice. They are lobbying to maintain intentionally restrictive laws that hold back the healthcare economy. Brace yourself for politicians explaining why we can’t afford tax cuts despite a healthy surplus.

**In politics, nothing moves unless it is pushed.** MCPP won’t just publish our _Blueprint_. We will push and push hard. Mississippi’s future is too important to let bad politics get in the way.

**Mississippi could be on the cusp of transformative changes. If we keep going, we will not only no longer be 50th, but we could become – like Tennessee or Alabama – a state that young people want to move to, not leave.**

To get your copy of our Blueprint , click [here!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=bb4bb2a934&e=5703353baa)
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=82414f37af&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO
---
|
---
| | _ _
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013
[Add us to your address book](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/vcard?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://mcpp-c58f.mailchimpsites.com/manage/preferences?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&e=5703353baa&c=86fc8a0ad8) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&t=b&e=5703353baa&c=86fc8a0ad8).

name: Hillsdale College via Old Glory Today
subject: Who rules whom?
body:
The Constitution of “We the People” is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.

This sponsored message from Old Glory Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | |
---
| | [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/WtsP0hScY9uKcNLrJU5aNw~~/AARclAA~~~)
---
| | | Dear Fellow American, Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its [**National Referendum on the Administrative State**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/WtsP0hScY9uKcNLrJU5aNw~~/AARclAA~~~).

Responding will only take a few minutes. Your input as an informed patriot is needed right away...because **our country is facing a constitutional crisis**.

It's a crisis that involves a fundamental political question: **who rules whom?**

The Constitution established a limited government in which the **people** ruled through their elected officials. Under this form of government, America became the freest and most prosperous nation in history.

Today, however, our constitutional system is one that increasingly exists in name only. **The Constitution of "We the People" is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.** This means we live in an era of growing bureaucratic despotism, manifested in an administrative state that bypasses the Constitution, rejects the separation of powers, and replaces the rule of law with regulations.

**But we can 't just TALK about the problem. We also have to TAKE ACTION--to DO SOMETHING about it, while there's still time to save our country. **

That is why I'm writing to you today.

Hillsdale College is committed to returning to the Constitution of "We the People"--before it's too late.

To do that, I need your input. What are your opinions on this topic--knowing that we can propose SOLUTIONS that will restore liberty?

**Please let me know your thoughts today by answering the National Referendum on the Administrative State using this secure link:**

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/WtsP0hScY9uKcNLrJU5aNw~~/AARclAA~~~)**

When you send in your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

The rise of the administrative state is a grave threat to our country.

To counter this threat, Hillsdale sponsors several educational outreach efforts--including _Imprimis_ , free online courses, programs on our Washington, D.C., campus, and a growing network of classical K-12 schools and homeschoolers--to teach citizens about the meaning of the Constitution and the importance of our founding principles of liberty.

Your gift of at least $15--or even $25, $35, $50, $100, or more if you're especially motivated to TAKE ACTION--will help us to expand these educational initiatives that are reaching and teaching millions.

Your support of these efforts is essential because Hillsdale refuses to take ONE PENNY of government funding.

So please complete your **National Referendum on the Administrative State** and consider giving your most generous tax-deductible contribution.

Thank you for being on the side of the Constitution and liberty!

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

**P.S.** I look forward to receiving your opinions about what's happening in our nation. And I hope that you can support our work.

So please complete the **National Referendum on the Administrative State** using this secure link:

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/WtsP0hScY9uKcNLrJU5aNw~~/AARclAA~~~)**

As soon as you respond, we will begin your FREE lifetime subscription to _Imprimis_ , Hillsdale's digest of liberty that you will receive via email ten times a year. If you'd like, you can also sign up to receive the publication through postal mail.

Educating millions about the Constitution to counter the threat from the administrative state is a monumental task. That's why when you complete your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Thank you for your time and attention--and for your commitment to liberty.
---
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

Old Glory Today
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_

clean_body: The Constitution of “We the People” is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens. This sponsored message from Conservatives Give is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | | | | | | | Dear Fellow American, Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its . Responding will only take a few minutes. Your input as an informed patriot is needed right away...because our country is facing a constitutional crisis. It's a crisis that involves a fundamental political question: who rules whom? The Constitution established a limited government in which the people ruled through their elected officials. Under this form of government, America became the freest and most prosperous nation in history. Today, however, our constitutional system is one that increasingly exists in name only. The Constitution of "We the People" is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens. This means we live in an era of growing bureaucratic despotism, manifested in an administrative state that bypasses the Constitution, rejects the separation of powers, and replaces the rule of law with regulations. But we can 't just TALK about the problem. We also have to TAKE ACTION--to DO SOMETHING about it, while there's still time to save our country. That is why I'm writing to you today. Hillsdale College is committed to returning to the Constitution of "We the People"--before it's too late. To do that, I need your input. What are your opinions on this topic--knowing that we can propose SOLUTIONS that will restore liberty? Please let me know your thoughts today by answering the National Referendum on the Administrative State using this secure link: When you send in your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift. The rise of the administrative state is a grave threat to our country. To counter this threat, Hillsdale sponsors several educational outreach efforts--including Imprimis , free online courses, programs on our Washington, D.C., campus, and a growing network of classical K-12 schools and homeschoolers--to teach citizens about the meaning of the Constitution and the importance of our founding principles of liberty. Your gift of at least $15--or even $25, $35, $50, $100, or more if you're especially motivated to TAKE ACTION--will help us to expand these educational initiatives that are reaching and teaching millions. Your support of these efforts is essential because Hillsdale refuses to take ONE PENNY of government funding. So please complete your National Referendum on the Administrative State and consider giving your most generous tax-deductible contribution. Thank you for being on the side of the Constitution and liberty! Warm regards, Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844 P.S. I look forward to receiving your opinions about what's happening in our nation. And I hope that you can support our work. So please complete the National Referendum on the Administrative State using this secure link: As soon as you respond, we will begin your FREE lifetime subscription to Imprimis , Hillsdale's digest of liberty that you will receive via email ten times a year. If you'd like, you can also sign up to receive the publication through postal mail. Educating millions about the Constitution to counter the threat from the administrative state is a monumental task. That's why when you complete your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift. Thank you for your time and attention--and for your commitment to liberty. | | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA Conservatives Give PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: Senator Jennifer B. Boysko
subject: Boysko Bulletin: I want to know what's on your mind- Upcoming public hearings
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/102624/images/Bulletin.jpeg]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/102624/images/IMG_3402-3.jpg]
Boysko Bulletin: I want to know what's on your mind
Dear Peter,
Happy New Year! My team and I always enjoy hearing from constituents about your concerns. We have public meetings later this week specifically so that my colleagues and I can listen to you and to learn what is most important to you pertaining to Virginia's public policy initiatives this week. I've included information about how to participate so that you don't miss out.
And as always, I will be sending weekly updates during the session to keep you informed.
Additionally, while many of us are fortunately surrounded by loved ones and the holiday season is a time for joy and connection, for some it can be a difficult time of the year. I've provided some important information with links to resources for those who might need it below as well.
In closing for now, I wish you and you loved ones Happy New Year! I hope that you have time to rest and enjoy your family and friends. I will be returning to Richmond on Sunday January 6th ready to work as we kick off the 2025 Virginia General Assembly Session.
Kindest regards,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/102624/images/2024%20February%20Images/electronic%20signature.png]
Public Hearings are Opportunities to Share Your Views
Watch and/or Comment at the January 3, 2025 Public Hearings on the Governor’s Proposed Amendments to the 2024-2026 Biennial State Budget
All public hearings will be held virtually on Friday, January 3rd, 2025 at 10 am. There will be four separate virtual hearings with legislators from each region. I will be participating in the Northern Virginia public hearing. Public registration to speak at one of the public hearings will occur on Thursday, January 2, 2025. There is more information on the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee Website [https://sfac.virginia.gov/] .
Those persons wishing to speak may register to speak the day before each virtual hearing from 8:00 am. through 4:00 pm.
Speakers will be taken in the order of registration. Each person may register only one speaker at a time and only sign-up to speak at one of the hearings. Speakers are asked to limit their comments to three minutes or less. Speakers representing groups and organizations should consolidate remarks to reduce duplication. Persons unable to attend may submit their comments electronically [https://tinyurl.com/5ymczt9c] . People with disabilities may request reasonable accommodations by calling (804) 698-7480. All requests for reasonable accommodations must be received by 5:00 p.m., Thursday December 26, 2024. Requested arrangements for sign language interpreters, received by December 26th, will be provided via video format.
The Public Hearings will be livestreamed on the Virginia General Assembly Website [https://tinyurl.com/27b8m2fp] .
You Can Comment at the Fairfax County Pre Session Legislative Hearing
Fairfax County General Assembly Delegation’s Pre-2025 Session Public Hearing
The Fairfax County delegation to the General Assembly will hold a public hearing regarding the upcoming 2025 session on Saturday, January 4 , at 9 a.m. in the Board Auditorium at the Fairfax County Government Center, 12000 Government Center Parkway, Fairfax with parking available in front of the Government Center Building. Fairfax County is assisting with the logistics of the delegation’s event. Fairfax County residents who want to sign up in advance to speak at the hearing should register online using this form [https://fairfaxcounty-639180.workflowcloud.com/forms/8cea202c-ee90-47e7-aa2f-cd318a57cf68] or contact the Department of Clerk Services at 703-324-3151, TTY 711, by noon on Thursday, January 2 . Additional guidelines for those interested in speaking at the public forum are as follows:
Only Fairfax County residents may register to speak.
Eligible residents requiring assistance will be helped as needed.
Speaking slots will be numbered based on a first come, first served allocation.
Fairfax County residents may register to speak as an individual or on behalf of an organization serving County residents.
Requests for ADA accommodations should be made as soon as possible but no later than 48 hours before the public hearing.
Organizations are encouraged to limit their presentation to no more than 3 speakers.
Each speaker will be allocated three minutes to address the Delegation.
Speakers must be present when called to the podium, and people who miss their turn will be added to the end of the list of speakers.
Written testimony may also be submitted via email to LegislativeTeam@fairfaxcounty.gov [LegislativeTeam@fairfaxcounty.gov] .
The Chair reserves the right to modify the guidelines on site for the smooth operation of the public forum.
The hearing will be televised live on Fairfax County Television Channel 16 (Channel 1016 in HD on Cox; Channel 16 on Verizon or Comcast), and can also be viewed on-line through the Channel 16 stream.
Reminder: Weapons Prohibited in County Buildings, Parks, at Special Events [https://www.fairfaxcounty.gov/news/board-votes-prohibit-weapons-county-buildings-parks-special-events]
Point of contact for other questions or requests for ADA accommodations: Arielle McAloon (703-324-2649, TTY 711) or Jennifer Van Ee (703-324-2640, TTY 711).
Further information on the public hearing is available from members of the Fairfax County legislative delegation. Contact information for individual delegation members is available on the Virginia General Assembly web page at http://whosmy.virginiageneralassembly.gov/ [http://whosmy.virginiageneralassembly.gov/]
Legislative Updates
How to Track Legislation
The website where you can find my legislation once the bills are introduced and track their progress has been updated. Check it out at https://lis.virginia.gov/ [https://lis.virginia.gov/] . By selecting “Delegates and Senators” and then my name, you will find Senator Boysko's page. You will see the committees I serve on and my Chief Patron, Chief Co-patron and Co-patron legislation.
Committee pages are where you will find when committees meet, their dockets, and what bills the committees are considering. The page for each bill lists the committee referral and there is a drop down menu that takes you to the committee page to figure out when the bill will be presented. We often get short notice. If you support one or more of my bills, email SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov [SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov] and we will add you to our stakeholder list and notify you when the bill(s) will be heard. We are very busy in Richmond but will do our best. Your support is very important to the success of our legislation.
Constituent Services
Addiction and Recovery Council
As a member of the Addiction and Recovery Council [https://bit.ly/3JEyG76] , we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery. Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. The Chris Atwood Foundation can help you get started [https://www.thecaf.org/] .
Reach Out for Mental Health Support
The 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline [https://tinyurl.com/2jmjetw6] is available nationwide. You can also find support for yourself and family members by reaching out to NAMI [https://bit.ly/3LJuRvu] or Mental Health America [https://bit.ly/3wIafQ7] .
* The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration National Helpline: 1-800-662-4357
* National Drug Helpline: 1-844-289-0879
* National Institute of Mental Health Information Resource Center: 1-866-615-6464
* Partnership for Drug-Free Kids: 1-855-378-4373
Unemployment Assistance and Resources for Job Searchers
For those eligible for benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. I encourage you to seek support for finding a job from Virginia Career Works [https://bit.ly/3xe2WgK] . They help identify training programs, job opportunities, and help with resumes, emails, and interviewing skills. My constituents report good results working with VCW. Please reach out if you need help with a VEC claim [https://tinyurl.com/ajw3hm3j] .
Help Paying Bills
If you are struggling to pay bills, please visit Virginia’s CommonHelp website [https://bit.ly/3301PnM] to figure out if you qualify for assistance.
You can also reach out to your county - your local government will review your financial situation and connect you with resources. In Fairfax County, contact Coordinated Services [https://bit.ly/2Gb4qmi] .
Here [https://bit.ly/3sFi0l3] are additional resources for food. If you have lost your health insurance, please visit coverva.gov [https://bit.ly/3mnEDu5] to figure out if you are eligible for Medicaid or for help with Affordable Care Act insurance.
Saturday and Sunday, January 19 from 10:30 am - 1:30 pm/ MLK Birthday Celebration Especially for Youth - RCC [https://restoncommunitycenter.com/event/mlk-birthday-celebration-especially-for-youth-3/]
We welcome notices about events that are free or very low cost.
Keep in Touch
To contact us to arrange a call or meeting, please email SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov [SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov] or call 703-437-0086. We are in Richmond from January 8 through February 22, 2025. My Senate team includes Chief of Staff Karen Harrison, Director of Constituent Services Kathy Shupe, Session Aide Gillie Cuda, and Legislative Aide Juliet Morro. Friend me on Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/JenniferBBoysko] and follow me on X [https://bit.ly/3uLhVhC] . You can also learn more about me at my website JenniferBoysko.com.
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe.
Friends of Jennifer Boysko
PO Box 247
Herndon, VA 20172
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/LakmKKqoaEa-z1agKTAOVw2?unsubscribedata=xm4ktRrsOYjeK3WtCP9xzFffxcLNhq0FRkbxCB8tuM4x5hluCwbwtecPeaNwno1V6v1YNEyBA%2BYfytxnFxU7Chy4ZvWICTPjTWLVCefo%2BC5B8aL3UVbSx%2BTQeKWVEaUKaNb8%2BYc7mh%2B4KPoUB15zD832kIDNvgELJcNA2pmzjfiJQpavFlVApPW7pgXUlb%2BDFkeMCC74ureEIpHySeGlJlexWTo4vwR8oUDkqm0%2FCN2Y4e859nm3%2BPU0uteNzHD0jm72vEn7Hsm0VUJHZTex0kzRgzra2%2B0vBY3XDVpEEEArH0wau9GNmWKw6DBPsmzuDHGbMEWFnMwEj5rGJuJJpNgaOg96zzjyYcCg5FDbGiFR%2F0pKeL4u4jpZjDY0jluxiloN%2Fv1O15CL7%2B%2Fph1FJ14nrzy%2FEE1%2BTLEe7uDrAK8wfl%2BzTPnfvQm1liQxHcUfF .

name: Team Sykes
subject: Happy New Year!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emilia Sykes for Congress
body:
From all of us at Sykes HQ:

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99675569/520562247/985656371?nd&amount=10&refcode=em_msg_2025.01.01_b1_ts_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMjkzYTMyNi00ZmM4LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I_LgOf9GzjtFbtABlpS0NADERlYzwOQHp12n9OqMmBI=) Happy New Year Peter! As we enter 2025, all of us here at Sykes HQ would like to wish you a happy and healthy New Year. This hardworking community is what drives my work everyday in Congress. So, I want to know what's most important to you. 2024 was a big year for our grassroots team. Together, we were able to push back against Emilia’s extreme opponent and ensure OH-13 stayed blue. None of it would have been possible without you and people just like you across Northeast Ohio. _Thank you._ Once again, Happy New Year Peter! _– Team Sykes_

| **A native of Akron, Ohio, Emilia Sykes has deep roots fighting for Ohioans. She learned from a young age how to use her voice and the power of the people to create change.** **In 2022, Congresswoman Sykes was elected to represent Ohio’s newly redrawn 13th Congressional District, winning one of the toughest races in the country._Emilia has cut through the divisiveness to deliver results._ **

name: Steve Kerrigan
subject: Happy New Year from MassDems!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/happynewyear_graphic.jpeg]
To all Massachusetts Democrats, family, friends, and loved ones–
Happy New Year! May this year bring you new adventures, time with those you care for most and the energy and determination to keep fighting the good fight.
I’m proud of what we accomplished as a community in 2024. We sent Senator Elizabeth Warren and our all-Democratic slate of Representatives back to Washington to keep fighting for MassDem values on Capitol Hill. We saw huge wins for Democratic State Legislators. We dispatched hundreds of volunteers to swing states to connect with voters, to resounding success. We came together, thousands strong, at our State Convention in June and then represented Massachusetts with pride at the Democratic National Convention in August. While the result of the national election was not what we all hoped for, when I look to this new year and the incoming administration, I am encouraged by the knowledge that Massachusetts Democrats will continue to be every bit as determined and driven to fight for what we believe in.
Like any other year, 2025 will bring its share of challenges and celebrations. We’ll mark them all with togetherness- uplifting one another through our shared fights and maintaining the values that drive us all. I am proud to be the Chair of such an incredible group of activists and I am confident that the year ahead will be our best year yet.
Best wishes,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Steve%20K%27s%20Signature.png]
Steve Kerrigan
Chair, Massachusetts Democratic Party
https://www.facebook.com/massdems [https://www.facebook.com/massdems] https://twitter.com/massdems [https://twitter.com/massdems] https://instagram.com/massdems [https://instagram.com/massdems] https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems [https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems]
Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee

11 Beacon St
Suite 410
Boston, MA 02108
United States

name: Gay Valimont
subject: I know your struggles. I’ve lived through them.

name: Congressman Raja Krishnamoorthi
subject: Happy New Year!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Raja for Congress
body:
Dear Peter,

Happy New Year! I hope this message finds you and your family well, and that you’ve been able to enjoy a restful holiday season surrounded by loved ones.

The New Year is an opportunity for a fresh start. It is a time we look forward to new opportunities, even as we reflect on our blessings from the previous year.

Thank you for your continued support and friendship– I wouldn’t be where I am today without you. May your 2025 be healthy, joyous, and prosperous!

Warmest regards,

Raja

[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP05/1/73169/images/srk%20holiday%20card%202024.PNG]

name: Hillsdale College via Full Mag News
subject: Who rules whom?
body:
The Constitution of “We the People” is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.

This sponsored message from Full Mag News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | |
---
| | [](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a4cdbf57d03046b1967be0dbd9977043&_e=OJ3Z8KC5d_i7kDF_mEDJ_Gr3bv671bv2BbyeB4jiqZ_AXC-CkHgHKp-gUCVudOvBuNC067-vuG8dt5tS6jcaAkzwf06eAMvkuS7XcP5pHyDcAKgQ5CkfBoMkCjRZhWv0j6drB39tlLYMOksrvD4yUNF9y2_EbuQ7-0KLJdNdidu5n3ajzZfUkUDfj1HjnpGlGK74ATADN9Wh9Go6_7eN39yxlXyOtO99BHDwVkicSxPzCaMGL83cyrPeApiTFMwPR8I7fmPTyUmb3b2N6CMToQS4dMTaHOiIlr7NZ0PhMZfZyXLuiEzJESyq1oUNSy9LF6Ji3S2rFkHk3yD78xXUwihgjFme28V2vbRkuWEM9uTZjb1Pu6Urvda9CLZq0Jp4iOdFh6q1Lkk0QMLU9oPxKnu474w8fTWqtJcHhwVH1FM%3D)
---
| | | Dear Fellow American, Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its [**National Referendum on the Administrative State**](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a4cdbf57d03046b1967be0dbd9977043&_e=OJ3Z8KC5d_i7kDF_mEDJ_Gr3bv671bv2BbyeB4jiqZ_AXC-CkHgHKp-gUCVudOvBuNC067-vuG8dt5tS6jcaAkzwf06eAMvkuS7XcP5pHyDcAKgQ5CkfBoMkCjRZhWv0j6drB39tlLYMOksrvD4yUNF9y2_EbuQ7-0KLJdNdidu5n3ajzZfUkUDfj1HjnpGlGK74ATADN9Wh9Go6_7eN39yxlXyOtO99BHDwVkicSxPzCaMGL83cyrPeApiTFMwPkoaILsInD4F1OFWzgTA1CERfGT--1rU3owqPdo_y_KrYaNgWf0SSVXweLigYrlNkayd1TF4IbvUredihIQE6GfnFp8Ui5LDyfPfO1AP-o9qeGaAPrw2HVr81Gsr0jwjvsAuTIt2IRoryEhDDznx84q6N3hBBTeBJkSeru9qlyV0%3D).

Responding will only take a few minutes. Your input as an informed patriot is needed right away...because **our country is facing a constitutional crisis**.

It's a crisis that involves a fundamental political question: **who rules whom?**

The Constitution established a limited government in which the **people** ruled through their elected officials. Under this form of government, America became the freest and most prosperous nation in history.

Today, however, our constitutional system is one that increasingly exists in name only. **The Constitution of "We the People" is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.** This means we live in an era of growing bureaucratic despotism, manifested in an administrative state that bypasses the Constitution, rejects the separation of powers, and replaces the rule of law with regulations.

**But we can 't just TALK about the problem. We also have to TAKE ACTION--to DO SOMETHING about it, while there's still time to save our country. **

That is why I'm writing to you today.

Hillsdale College is committed to returning to the Constitution of "We the People"--before it's too late.

To do that, I need your input. What are your opinions on this topic--knowing that we can propose SOLUTIONS that will restore liberty?

**Please let me know your thoughts today by answering the National Referendum on the Administrative State using this secure link:**

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a4cdbf57d03046b1967be0dbd9977043&_e=OJ3Z8KC5d_i7kDF_mEDJ_Gr3bv671bv2BbyeB4jiqZ_AXC-CkHgHKp-gUCVudOvBuNC067-vuG8dt5tS6jcaAkzwf06eAMvkuS7XcP5pHyDcAKgQ5CkfBoMkCjRZhWv0j6drB39tlLYMOksrvD4yUNF9y2_EbuQ7-0KLJdNdidu5n3ajzZfUkUDfj1HjnpGlGK74ATADN9Wh9Go6_7eN39yxlXyOtO99BHDwVkicSxPzCaMGL83cyrPeApiTFMwPM5b_sdLBUed6eFZrH9hGNKruKYXyiqKd2hUGPoJSnJ2sb7YuLH9rAbajfL3nSwQP9zmI0lGI6hP1iIyDh1Az9GDUyG6rChW0Ig7YL9bOrE8HxwGz8OzzJewFLEgqhUKsZRZtq8u3m3BPpf2sJDWDRpmHjiItGLYxl4ckZENQbPE%3D)**

When you send in your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

The rise of the administrative state is a grave threat to our country.

To counter this threat, Hillsdale sponsors several educational outreach efforts--including _Imprimis_ , free online courses, programs on our Washington, D.C., campus, and a growing network of classical K-12 schools and homeschoolers--to teach citizens about the meaning of the Constitution and the importance of our founding principles of liberty.

Your gift of at least $15--or even $25, $35, $50, $100, or more if you're especially motivated to TAKE ACTION--will help us to expand these educational initiatives that are reaching and teaching millions.

Your support of these efforts is essential because Hillsdale refuses to take ONE PENNY of government funding.

So please complete your **National Referendum on the Administrative State** and consider giving your most generous tax-deductible contribution.

Thank you for being on the side of the Constitution and liberty!

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

**P.S.** I look forward to receiving your opinions about what's happening in our nation. And I hope that you can support our work.

So please complete the **National Referendum on the Administrative State** using this secure link:

**[https://info.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a4cdbf57d03046b1967be0dbd9977043&_e=OJ3Z8KC5d_i7kDF_mEDJ_Gr3bv671bv2BbyeB4jiqZ_AXC-CkHgHKp-gUCVudOvBuNC067-vuG8dt5tS6jcaAkzwf06eAMvkuS7XcP5pHyDcAKgQ5CkfBoMkCjRZhWv0j6drB39tlLYMOksrvD4yUNF9y2_EbuQ7-0KLJdNdidu5n3ajzZfUkUDfj1HjnpGlGK74ATADN9Wh9Go6_7eN39yxlXyOtO99BHDwVkicSxPzCaMGL83cyrPeApiTFMwP67ExbyTUO4JhEZYRUlqdwzm6JBJuAgDXAvQvoivDZRYGkpN77G8tiClnFhq9N41OM8gJt0AZgmbhO8QLlbsncxNchzp0w8MZxHIcB69eLOjmHgz-gE3-bqzTNOZ65igcSxJPaIT4WohZHB6f90Gwl1BO-_ut2a7svpwCsZ8VoC4%3D)**

As soon as you respond, we will begin your FREE lifetime subscription to _Imprimis_ , Hillsdale's digest of liberty that you will receive via email ten times a year. If you'd like, you can also sign up to receive the publication through postal mail.

Educating millions about the Constitution to counter the threat from the administrative state is a monumental task. That's why when you complete your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Thank you for your time and attention--and for your commitment to liberty.
---
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

Full Mag News
---

Happy New Year, friend! I hope you’ve had a chance to rest and relax over the holidays with family and friends. 2024 was a tough year, but we all proved to be resilient and keep pushing the benchmark of progress. With people like you supporting my campaign, it’s been possible to accomplish all that we have. Last year, we oversaw an increase in economic growth, codified reproductive rights into our state constitution, and achieved so much more to ensure that Maryland is the state that serves. 2025 is going to be an upward battle, but we are equipped to take it on with folks like you by our side. So, from my family to yours, once again: Happy New Year! Elevate, Wes P.S. The fundraising freeze is in 7 days. We need your support now more than ever so we can keep powering through until April. clean_body: Happy New Year, friend! I hope you’ve had a chance to rest and relax over the holidays with family and friends. 2024 was a tough year, but we all proved to be resilient and keep pushing the benchmark of progress. With people like you supporting my campaign, it’s been possible to accomplish all that we have. Last year, we oversaw an increase in economic growth, codified reproductive rights into our state constitution, and achieved so much more to ensure that Maryland is the state that serves. 2025 is going to be an upward battle, but we are equipped to take it on with folks like you by our side. So, from my family to yours, once again: Happy New Year! Elevate, Wes P.S. The fundraising freeze is in 7 days. We need your support now more than ever so we can keep powering through until April. = | =

name: Tina Smith
subject: New year, new goals
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
I hope your 2025 is filled with family, friends, and lots of laughter! |
#
Hi there, wishing you a very Happy New Year! I hope your 2025 is filled with family, friends, and lots of laughter! I also wanted to take a moment to say thank you for stepping up during our end-of-quarter fundraising push last month. December can get busy, and I can’t tell you how much I appreciate you taking some time to read these emails, sign our petitions, and stay involved with our team. We have our work cut out for us in the new year, but I know that this team is capable of scoring some big wins when we all work together. I’m ready to lace up my sneakers and get to work — and I hope you’ll be there right alongside me taking up the tough fights. Once again, thank you. Wishing you and your loved ones a happy new year, and I’m looking forward to working with you this year! name: Tom Suozzi
subject: Happy New Year – My Op-Ed Today in the NY Times
body:
My New Year’s resolution is to continue to rise above partisanship | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/eaebd3ba75e7/happy-new-year-my-op-ed-today-in-the-ny-times?e=2d63ff009b)
---
| | | |

| Dear Friend, My New Year’s resolution is to continue to rise above partisanship and bickering, reject extremism and embrace common sense. Let’s work on border security, immigration reform, low inflation, economic stability and common ground on culture-war fights. That's good for America. [My op-ed in the Times](https://suozziforcongress2024.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1c0ad4a8bbd8df94a8b1718e5&id=b531060b24&e=2d63ff009b)
---
[View](https://suozziforcongress2024.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1c0ad4a8bbd8df94a8b1718e5&id=56d18d2a1c&e=2d63ff009b)
|

# Let's Try Something Different in How We Deal with Trump
_By Tom Suozzi_
President-elect Donald Trump and the Republicans have managed to sell themselves as the party of change. It worked: They will soon control the presidency, Congress and, in essence, the Supreme Court. But to change and fix America requires both parties to work together. As a Democratic member of Congress, I know my party will be tempted to hold fast against Mr. Trump at every turn: uniting against his bills, blocking his nominees and grinding the machinery of the House and the Senate to a halt.
That would be a mistake. Only by working together to find compromise on parts of Mr. Trump’s agenda can we make progress for Americans who are clearly demanding change in the economy, immigration, crime and other top issues.
I’m no dupe: Some of Mr. Trump’s actions offer little reassurance that he is ready to embrace the bipartisanship and compromise essential to a functioning democracy. His radical cabinet picks, such as the Project 2025 contributor Russell Vought and Matt Gaetz (now withdrawn); his last-minute demands on last month’s government funding bill; and the recent demonstrations of hubris, such as Republicans bringing Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy to Capitol Hill and refusing to include Democrats in the meetings, offer no reason for optimism about compromise. But if Mr. Trump wants to have a more effective presidency than he had in his first term, he needs to embrace his inner dealmaker and negotiate with the other party that holds just shy of half of the seats on Capitol Hill and key governorships around the country. And if he does that work, Democrats should meet him halfway rather than be the Party of No.I know many voters reject my party’s significant leftward shift. So do I. But as a common-sense Democrat who won in a district that Mr. Trump also won, I am certain our closely divided electorate would rather have bipartisan solutions than political gridlock. After Mr. Trump almost caused a federal shutdown with the funding bill antics, the government was able to stay open only through Democrats joining with some Republicans to pass compromise legislation. Americans shouldn’t have to hold their breath to see if we’ll do the right thing together.
The election was a mandate. But it wasn’t for one-party rule — Mr. Trump won with less than 50 percent of the popular vote, and Republicans have thin majorities in the House and the Senate. But as I see it, the results of the 2024 campaign were a mandate for border security, immigration reform, low inflation, economic stability and common ground on culture-war fights. That’s good for America. So let’s make that our shared agenda in 2025. There are a majority of votes for all of it.And let’s try something different when it comes to the president-elect.Since the day Mr. Trump announced his candidacy at the tower bearing his name almost 10 years ago, many politicians, pundits, activists and members of the news media have detailed every one of his failings and missteps. Every word he’s ever spoken has been criticized. Yet he just won again. People are exhausted by the endless finger-pointing, nit-picking and daily battling for political advantage. They want leaders to work together to get things done.
Some members of my party and left-leaning advocacy groups are now branding themselves as the leaders of a national “resistance” movement, reflexively opposing ideas from the incoming administration. That’s a bad idea.
Resistance has a role. During the prior Trump presidency, I resisted his efforts to undo the Affordable Care Act and to deport the Dreamers. And we can and should continue to resist Mr. Trump’s efforts to retaliate against his perceived political enemies by weaponizing the Justice Department, his pledge to gut policies that combat climate change and protect our environment, and his threat to bring the United States back to an isolationist view of the world. To lead effectively, we must find common ground, build consensus and offer solutions. Democrats must resist when necessary, but our general outlook must be to go beyond resistance and articulate a vision that inspires.
For instance, while it is essential to secure the border and deport criminals, we must also reform the broken asylum system and modernize legal immigration to provide pathways to legalization for Dreamers, Temporary Protected Status recipients and farmworkers. Immigration must be governed by the rule of law while protecting immigrant families from fear and ensuring our economy is kept stable while treating human beings like human beings.
Under President Biden, Democrats refocused national policy on rebuilding the middle class by creating solid job opportunities with the Infrastructure Law and promoting manufacturing under the CHIPs Act. Unfortunately, we failed to communicate the effort effectively. While Mr. Biden was often quoted saying, “It’s time to grow the economy from the bottom up and the middle out,” no one really understood that he was talking about creating more solidly middle-class jobs and putting forth a real policy to do just that.
Republicans claim they are for working families, but it is Democrats who support an increase in the minimum wage, adoption of the union-friendly PRO Act and a robust enhancement of the child tax credit. Voters need to hear that.Democrats cannot abandon our zeal to combat climate change. At the same time, let’s balance our commitment to environmental protection with pragmatic measures that safeguard affordable utility bills and manageable costs at the pump. Let’s move beyond the relentless attacks on widely held religious values while ensuring that the rights, safety and dignity of all are upheld. And Democrats should be supportive of efforts to make government more efficient and effective, but we must fiercely defend and advocate the strengthening of Social Security, Medicare and the Affordable Care Act.
Both parties should seek new ideas and leaders to demonstrate a willingness to break away from the restrictive orthodox ideologies of some of the more extreme members of the Democrats’ Progressive Caucus and the Republicans’ Freedom Caucus, who limit our ability to seek common ground and get things done.This time in history is both a warning and an opportunity. My New Year’s resolution is to rise above partisanship and bickering, reject extremism and embrace common sense, and keep building relationships with Republicans and Democrats to get things done. I’ll work with anyone who wants to solve problems and make things better for people, but I’ll never abandon my values. If Republicans and Democrats choose the path of division and overreach, they will deepen the partisan divides that have already weakened our democracy. But if they embrace bipartisanship and cooperation, 2025 can be a better year for all Americans. We have to remember that the ultimate goal of government should be serving the American people, not our respective parties.
---

Because this new boresighter will let you zero in on your target with pinpoint accuracy, all without firing a single shot!

In fact, you can achieve sniper precision without wasting a single round of ammo or even leaving your home!

Introducing...

Codename: **The Boar**

Once reserved for military sharpshooters, this advanced sighting technology is now available for civilian use.

Take a look: The Boar is compatible with all calibers from .17 HMR to .50 Cal, requires no live fire, and gives you a perfect zero in just 10 seconds!

Thanks to an exclusive deal, I’m able to offer The Boar to you at an insane [**36% OFF discount...**](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/19562/6374453/14962894c136fbe879eeb20ff1ba0a0d06b2938f/229e0072715987633cb7274e6719d27a9ae88665)

While supplies last, I’m throwing in free adapters and free shipping!

[**Grab your MCG Boar Laser Boresighter before I run out.**](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/19562/6374453/14962894c136fbe879eeb20ff1ba0a0d06b2938f/229e0072715987633cb7274e6719d27a9ae88665)

In Liberty, Oli Fischer

P.S. Imagine getting the best zero or your entire shooting life... without firing a single round.

[](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/19562/6374453/eb58f24f91e65cbcd0ef3158eb68adfef8347121/229e0072715987633cb7274e6719d27a9ae88665)**Zero ammo. Zero range trips. Zero frustration.**

This little device allows you to get a sub-MOA zero on any rifle, carbine, handgun or even shotgun -- 100% safely and from the comfort of your own home.

[**Get your MCG Boar 37% OFF here (Free S &H)**](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/19562/6374453/14962894c136fbe879eeb20ff1ba0a0d06b2938f/229e0072715987633cb7274e6719d27a9ae88665)

Dear Friend,
As we welcome 2025, I wish you a healthy and happy New Year with faith in the future, hope in our dreams and love in our hearts.
Working together, we will Save Our Health Care, defend our most vulnerable communities, protect our planet and advance our freedoms.
Thank you for all your support.
Happy New Year,
NANCY

Team, Happy New Year! I’m looking forward to officially becoming Delaware’s next Senator in just a few days! One of my New Year’s resolutions is to ensure that I am placing my constituents’ best interests at the forefront of all my decisions in the Senate. With leadership changing in Congress and the White House, there is a lot of uncertainty heading into 2025. **But one thing that is certain is my commitment to improving the lives of each and every Delawarean, regardless of their politics.** It’s easy to feel hopeless when we look at the changes facing our nation, but I hope we all make a resolution to stay hopeful and to do everything we can to fight back against any threat to our democracy and our freedom. [**To help me reach my constituents with my message of Bright Hope, I rely on support from people like you. One of my New Year’s resolutions is to ensure that I am placing my constituents’ best interests at the forefront of all my decisions in the Senate. With leadership changing in Congress and the White House, there is a lot of uncertainty heading into 2025. But one thing that is certain is my commitment to improving the lives of each and every Delawarean, regardless of their politics. | [](https://substack.com/redirect/36d9259e-dd1e-47a0-b48a-517abb9423c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Muslims are good and simple people.
We believe in God.
We love our land and the earth.
We raise our children to be good stewards and members of their family and society, loving and responsible.
We nurture the earth and cultivate wholesome abundance.
We refrain from vanity or excess.
We share what we have.
We take care of the needy.
We plan and provide for the future.
We do not betray our principles for personal gain.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tZWxpc3NhNGNvbmdyZXNzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.TOUMCdehUyDVVGSW89oPZ-mqCVJrDNXKEJDwx1yGqIc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=153641787)
In commerce, we are honest.
In labor, we are hardworking.
In family life, we are dutiful, cheerful, affectionate, responsible, and kind.
To our neighbors, we are peaceful, helpful, and friendly.
To our colleagues, we are reliable and trustworthy, and known for seeking knowledge.
In society, we are the ones who can be leaned on, who cannot be bought, and whose word can be trusted.
To the people who look up to us, we are the mentors and wise examples, and the ones who one should not try to manipulate.
To the tyrant, we are the implacable river that cuts through the mountain.
To the oppressed, we are the steadfast supporter, protector, and defender.
To the children, we are loving parents, aunts, uncles, and teachers whose classroom is this whole good world.
To the elders, we are stable and loving companions, caregivers, and trustworthy inheritors.
To each other, we are easy and steadfast allies, brothers and sisters who wish each other the peace of God many times a day.
To God, hopefully, we are a pleasure - and if Allah sees fit to improve the world through our efforts, we consider that our greatest honor.
To humanity, we are meant to be a kindness. To establish justice, to insure domestic tranquility, to provide for the common defense - these are things that we as Muslims, not just Americans, are instructed to do as individuals, daily and in all ways, forever.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=153641787&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzNjQxNzg3LCJpYXQiOjE3MzU3NDQ3MzYsImV4cCI6MTczODMzNjczNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0R-w-MpdwyX6mKsciBmvOPxVvI8--ulmj1904uOAaug)
Anything that benefits humanity, we support.
Anything that damages, destroys, or demeans humanity - or human beings - we oppose.
We treat prisoners with dignity and respect. We share our food. We do not torture.
We do not kill noncombatants. We do not poison wells. We do not destroy infrastructure. We do not hurt children.
We consider ourselves answerable to a higher power. One whose nature is both blessed mercy and perfect justice, before whom all oppressors shall be held accountable by those they oppressed.
In our religion and way of life, the rights of people must be respected even before the rights of God. Debts to people must be paid before charity can be given - and charity is a pillar of our faith, and part of our path to heaven.

We are implacable fighters, but we give grace the moment an enemy surrenders.
We do justice to all people, without racial, social, economic, or any other form of superiority. The only superiority we recognize is that of morality, charity, wisdom, kindness, character, and good works.
We strive against oppression - not to inflict it, not to be complicit in it, not to enable it, and not to submit when it is inflicted upon ourselves.
We celebrate the beauty and generosity of the natural world. We celebrate the goodness of each other. We celebrate the health and character of our children.
[Give a gift subscription](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tZWxpc3NhNGNvbmdyZXNzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.TOUMCdehUyDVVGSW89oPZ-mqCVJrDNXKEJDwx1yGqIc?&gift=true)
We seek knowledge, and consider it our duty to advance knowledge in ways that benefit humanity.
We avoid vice - any practices that harm ourselves or others, including drugs, alcohol, gambling, rampant sex, unhealthy food, dishonest or dishonorable work, manipulative or fraudulent business practices, and financial systems that rely on exploitation.

We try to be a haven for the needy.
We work hard to provide for ourselves and our households. We work to build up good systems and stores of value for the future. We work to generate resources to put towards good work. We work hard so that we and our good wealth can be a benefit to our families, communities, countries, and the broader world.
We believe that to take one innocent life is as if you have killed all of humanity; and to save one innocent life is as if you have saved all of humanity.

We believe in love and affection between spouses, and in harmony, respect, and kind treatment. If this is no longer achievable, we believe in peaceful and honorable divorce, without acrimony or injustice. We believe in providing for children and their mothers, and keeping them together. We value highly the blessing and charity of caring for orphans.
We structure our society to provide optimum support and opportunity for both men and women. We were the first to provide formal and universal rights to children. We were the first to uplift women in commerce, women's right to divorce, women's property and inheritance rights, women in leadership, women as scholars and knowledge seekers, women as scientists, authors, and the founders of libraries and universities.
We see our men as human beings - not hypermasculinized and desensitized machines to kill, or accumulate wealth, or be cannon fodder.
We do not see weapons of war as suitable for profit.
[Share "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tZWxpc3NhNGNvbmdyZXNzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS8_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1zdWJzdGFjayZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PXNoYXJlJmFjdGlvbj1zaGFyZSIsInAiOjE1MzY0MTc4NywicyI6MzI2MDExMSwiZiI6dHJ1ZSwidSI6OTA1MDExOTIsImlhdCI6MTczNTc0NDczNiwiZXhwIjoxNzM4MzM2NzM2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMCIsInN1YiI6ImxpbmstcmVkaXJlY3QifQ.C7ej18xDHbBw_dm7q6_SSB6QLN5W_XJbhjoaLDRWxPw?)
We have peace with our lives. We give thanks to God whether in times of ease or hardship - ease for its pleasure and enjoyment, hardship for how it strengthens and refines us. Always we ask that God help us to do the right thing, and use us to bring about good things in this world.
We have peace with our deaths. We believe that death will come at a time and in a manner that no one - not ourselves, nor anyone else - can affect. And to us, death is the road back to reunion with the greatest source of love, comfort, guidance, and inspiration the human heart can ever know. So we do not fear death, and we do not fear those who threaten to inflict it upon us.
We pray five (or more) times a day. In those moments of dedicated surrender and loving union, we find peace. The peace that surpasses understanding - the peace that the human heart longs for, and constantly seeks to find.
In those prayers, we offer ourselves up to the ultimate source of grace, wisdom, and goodness, and we ask for guidance. We give ourselves to the mercy of our highest power, in love, gratitude, and complete trust, and we ask to be guided towards goodness and away from harm. We also pray for our family, our neighbors, our teachers, our leaders, our friends, those who are suffering, and those who are without hope.
We seek to nurture the health and success of everyone, without exception - and - without undue enablement. We believe in working for our own living, contributing something valuable to the world whether great or small - not in begging, or living on the government's largesse because it always comes at someone else's cost.
We love one another, and we love humanity, and we love God, and we love the earth.
We do not cut down fruit trees even in times of war. In fact, the Qur'an teaches us that **even on the day the world comes to an end, if you happen to have a good tree sapling in your hand, you should plant it**.
We believe that our ultimate purpose and responsibility is to bring about the intertwined blessings of justice and peace upon the earth.
We love our children.
We are Muslim...
...and unless you are wholeheartedly committed to exploitation, oppression, war crimes, theft, murder, destruction, violence, self-betrayal, complicity, cowardice, corruption, blindness, ignorance, arrogance, injustice, and pain...
...we are not your enemy.
> Salaam aleikum -
> may the peace of God be with you.
Happy 2025.
Let's make this the year that turns the tide.
