clean_body: Thoughts on what it means to be Muslim. Because the world needs to know. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | Send the gift of "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry this holiday season| | | ? Thoughts on what it means to be Muslim. Because the world needs to know. | | | Jan 1 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Muslims are good and simple people. We believe in God. We love our land and the earth. We raise our children to be good stewards and members of their family and society, loving and responsible. We nurture the earth and cultivate wholesome abundance. We refrain from vanity or excess. We share what we have. We take care of the needy. We plan and provide for the future. We do not betray our principles for personal gain. In commerce, we are honest. In labor, we are hardworking. In family life, we are dutiful, cheerful, affectionate, responsible, and kind. To our neighbors, we are peaceful, helpful, and friendly. To our colleagues, we are reliable and trustworthy, and known for seeking knowledge. In society, we are the ones who can be leaned on, who cannot be bought, and whose word can be trusted. To the people who look up to us, we are the mentors and wise examples, and the ones who one should not try to manipulate. To the tyrant, we are the implacable river that cuts through the mountain. To the oppressed, we are the steadfast supporter, protector, and defender. To the children, we are loving parents, aunts, uncles, and teachers whose classroom is this whole good world. To the elders, we are stable and loving companions, caregivers, and trustworthy inheritors. To each other, we are easy and steadfast allies, brothers and sisters who wish each other the peace of God many times a day. To God, hopefully, we are a pleasure - and if Allah sees fit to improve the world through our efforts, we consider that our greatest honor. To humanity, we are meant to be a kindness. To establish justice, to insure domestic tranquility, to provide for the common defense - these are things that we as Muslims, not just Americans, are instructed to do as individuals, daily and in all ways, forever. Anything that benefits humanity, we support. Anything that damages, destroys, or demeans humanity - or human beings - we oppose. We treat prisoners with dignity and respect. We share our food. We do not torture. We do not kill noncombatants. We do not poison wells. We do not destroy infrastructure. We do not hurt children. We consider ourselves answerable to a higher power. One whose nature is both blessed mercy and perfect justice, before whom all oppressors shall be held accountable by those they oppressed. In our religion and way of life, the rights of people must be respected even before the rights of God. Debts to people must be paid before charity can be given - and charity is a pillar of our faith, and part of our path to heaven. We are implacable fighters, but we give grace the moment an enemy surrenders. We do justice to all people, without racial, social, economic, or any other form of superiority. The only superiority we recognize is that of morality, charity, wisdom, kindness, character, and good works. We strive against oppression - not to inflict it, not to be complicit in it, not to enable it, and not to submit when it is inflicted upon ourselves. We celebrate the beauty and generosity of the natural world. We celebrate the goodness of each other. We celebrate the health and character of our children. We seek knowledge, and consider it our duty to advance knowledge in ways that benefit humanity. We avoid vice - any practices that harm ourselves or others, including drugs, alcohol, gambling, rampant sex, unhealthy food, dishonest or dishonorable work, manipulative or fraudulent business practices, and financial systems that rely on exploitation. We try to be a haven for the needy. We work hard to provide for ourselves and our households. We work to build up good systems and stores of value for the future. We work to generate resources to put towards good work. We work hard so that we and our good wealth can be a benefit to our families, communities, countries, and the broader world. We believe that to take one innocent life is as if you have killed all of humanity; and to save one innocent life is as if you have saved all of humanity. We believe in love and affection between spouses, and in harmony, respect, and kind treatment. If this is no longer achievable, we believe in peaceful and honorable divorce, without acrimony or injustice. We believe in providing for children and their mothers, and keeping them together. We value highly the blessing and charity of caring for orphans. We structure our society to provide optimum support and opportunity for both men and women. We were the first to provide formal and universal rights to children. We were the first to uplift women in commerce, women's right to divorce, women's property and inheritance rights, women in leadership, women as scholars and knowledge seekers, women as scientists, authors, and the founders of libraries and universities. We see our men as human beings - not hypermasculinized and desensitized machines to kill, or accumulate wealth, or be cannon fodder. We do not see weapons of war as suitable for profit. ? We have peace with our lives. We give thanks to God whether in times of ease or hardship - ease for its pleasure and enjoyment, hardship for how it strengthens and refines us. Always we ask that God help us to do the right thing, and use us to bring about good things in this world. We have peace with our deaths. We believe that death will come at a time and in a manner that no one - not ourselves, nor anyone else - can affect. And to us, death is the road back to reunion with the greatest source of love, comfort, guidance, and inspiration the human heart can ever know. So we do not fear death, and we do not fear those who threaten to inflict it upon us. We pray five (or more) times a day. In those moments of dedicated surrender and loving union, we find peace. The peace that surpasses understanding - the peace that the human heart longs for, and constantly seeks to find. In those prayers, we offer ourselves up to the ultimate source of grace, wisdom, and goodness, and we ask for guidance. We give ourselves to the mercy of our highest power, in love, gratitude, and complete trust, and we ask to be guided towards goodness and away from harm. We also pray for our family, our neighbors, our teachers, our leaders, our friends, those who are suffering, and those who are without hope. We seek to nurture the health and success of everyone, without exception - and - without undue enablement. We believe in working for our own living, contributing something valuable to the world whether great or small - not in begging, or living on the government's largesse because it always comes at someone else's cost. We love one another, and we love humanity, and we love God, and we love the earth. We do not cut down fruit trees even in times of war. In fact, the Qur'an teaches us that even on the day the world comes to an end, if you happen to have a good tree sapling in your hand, you should plant it. We believe that our ultimate purpose and responsibility is to bring about the intertwined blessings of justice and peace upon the earth. We love our children. We are Muslim... ...and unless you are wholeheartedly committed to exploitation, oppression, war crimes, theft, murder, destruction, violence, self-betrayal, complicity, cowardice, corruption, blindness, ignorance, arrogance, injustice, and pain... ...we are not your enemy. Salaam aleikum - may the peace of God be with you. Happy 2025. Let's make this the year that turns the tide. "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry is a reader-supported publication. name: Marcus
subject: Let’s Keep Fighting for a Better Future
body:
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
Dear Friend,
As we welcome 2025, I want to thank you. Serving Upstate NY in Congress has been the honor of a lifetime. Together, we tackled some of the toughest challenges facing our nation—fighting the fentanyl crisis, protecting our farmlands, and passing over 40 bipartisan bills that deliver real results. We made an impact because we stayed focused—not on the noise, but on the mission.
On January 3rd, my chapter in Congress comes to a close. But make no mistake: my fight for our community, our values, and a stronger, safer America is just beginning. This is not an end—it’s a new beginning. I’m ready to double down on the work that matters most, and I need you by my side.
Your support has always been my greatest source of strength. As we step into this new year, let’s stand together and keep pushing for a better future.
---
[DONATE TODAY](https://marcforus.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7a5de207e774ea8a3851d76c&id=f39af17c01&e=a39a20e5dd)
| Thank you for your faith, your partnership, and your belief in what we can achieve. Wishing you and your loved ones a joyful, healthy, and blessed New Year.
With gratitude,Marc MolinaroCongressman

# [Demand the Senate Reject Trump's Dangerous and Unqualified Nominees](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcf31026f-227d-4e29-b3dd-1f64804e8ad0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

---
| | | Donald Trump has nominated a frightening number of wildly and dangerously unqualified candidates for cabinet positions -- and the Senate must reject them. These candidates are so awful that even some Republican senators have spoken out against them. And they have already derailed at least one of them (Matt Gaetz). Now they must have the guts to do more. **[Add your name, and tell the senate to reject them.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T97fc81a8-99f2-4770-ab13-09ab88e62333/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)** These are not candidates who simply have different political opinions, they are unhinged lunatics who would literally put our national security in jeopardy. And if even a handful of Republicans join Democrats in doing what is right for America, we can defeat them. [**Add your name: Demand the Senate Reject Trump's Dangerous and Unqualified Nominees**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tff9c38e4-661b-4765-a864-992b6b518340/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

subject: Trump prosecuting Jack Smith?!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
please don’t ignore this!
| **🚨 URGENT 🚨
Trump Has Sights Set on Prosecuting Political Rivals — Including Jack Smith ** Friend, despite Jack Smith announcing he’s stepping down, Donald Trump is STILL out for revenge. **He’s not only plotting to DESTROY all evidence against him, but PROSECUTE Jack Smith!** [**We need 99 more Democrats in your area to sign our letter thanking Jack Smith for his work and standing with him against these vile attacks! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85668?t=6&akid=18328%2E878179%2EH2bGAv) | [Donate $3 to sign the letter now »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85668?t=7&akid=18328%2E878179%2EH2bGAv)
---
To: Special Counsel Jack Smith
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: I stand with you. **Dear Special Counsel Jack Smith,**

**_I am FURIOUS that Trump is seeking to prosecute you!_**

Trump is running scared because he KNOWS you’re about to drop hundreds of pages of evidence detailing his treasonous crimes.

He’s even promising to be a _“dictator on day one,”_ hell-bent on avenging anyone who has held him accountable.

I know it isn’t easy fighting off Trump and the GOP’s escalating threats to take you down, so please know that Democrats across the country stand with you.

[**I can’t thank you enough for all of your amazing work over the years to bring Donald Trump to justice! So I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my gratitude and to keep electing incredible Democrats who will save our democracy from MAGA extremism »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85668?t=8&akid=18328%2E878179%2EH2bGAv) | [Donate $3 to sign the letter now »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/85668?t=9&akid=18328%2E878179%2EH2bGAv)
---
__X A grateful Democrat__
_The Voter Protection Project_
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=18328%2E878179%2EH2bGAv). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=18328%2E878179%2EH2bGAv).**

#
Peter, it’s Dick Durbin.
I just wanted to take a moment to say Happy New Year! I know you are spending time with family and friends, so I’ll make this quick.
2024 is behind us now, and we are staring down the changes coming this year. Donald Trump and his administration will officially be in charge in just a few weeks.
We know that the next four years will be tough, so I made a list of resolutions for 2025:
1. Fighting tooth and nail to prevent Donald Trump’s dangerous Project 2025 agenda from becoming reality.
2. Standing up for all Americans – no matter who they are or what they believe in.
3. Ensuring Democrats win at every level in 2025 and beyond.

[**If those three resolutions resonate with you, then start 2025 right by chipping in $5 or more to help us protect our fundamental freedoms.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99685272/520581791/-1445317843?amount=5&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.01_NYResolutions_B1_F1_S1_C1_Actives_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZGIwYTc3Yy01OWM4LWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDIyNDgyYTkzYWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KMJxrpN8Jb8FTXfcbDQNGAcR52OC81GZWZbp4WVt7mA=)

A lesson from four historical icons and how we use their example to overcome the onslaught of injustice in 2025

# [An Opening Thought on 2025](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=153866898&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzODY2ODk4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzU3NDc1NDksImV4cCI6MTczODMzOTU0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Phva1sLN3HUPBDflCG2ZQGLz0kTXXE8SWeezN3iNtok)
### A lesson from four historical icons and how we use their example to overcome the onslaught of injustice in 2025
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jan 1
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=153866898&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzODY2ODk4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczNTc0NzU0OSwiZXhwIjoxNzM4MzM5NTQ5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.81DP3ztL0o_H8OsNI_MGEL29h9d2tx5P5Y4kIYZjdB4&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=153866898&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzODY2ODk4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzU3NDc1NDksImV4cCI6MTczODMzOTU0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Phva1sLN3HUPBDflCG2ZQGLz0kTXXE8SWeezN3iNtok&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=153866898&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUzODY2ODk4LCJpYXQiOjE3MzU3NDc1NDksImV4cCI6MTczODMzOTU0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Phva1sLN3HUPBDflCG2ZQGLz0kTXXE8SWeezN3iNtok)
---
| | [](https://substack.._havtojlOuVtX9SUkgd8HjM4O-42LKF8il1vR40zPzM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/an-opening-thought-on-2025?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..-l17GGQRxs4zbckfIe-jpK5IhYM0NeRRDd6WlR8nTGM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=153866898)
Jimmy Carter's passing made me reflect on how young our nation is. Consider this.
* President Carter was born Oct 1, 1924 and died December 29, 2024, at the age of 100. When he was 32, the last Civil War veteran, Albert Henry Woolson, died.
* Albert Henry Woolson was born on Feb 11, 1850 and died on August 2, 1956, at the age of 106. When he was 19, the last Revolutionary War veteran, Daniel Frederick Bakeman, died.
* Daniel Frederick Bakeman, was born in 1759 and died on April 5, 1869, at the age of 109.

In short, a man who lived with us and died just days ago in 2024, was an adult when the last Civil War veteran died, who himself was an adult when the last American Revolution veteran died, who himself was born in 1759--before this nation even existed.
But there's a fourth person we must acknowledge, without whom perhaps the aforementioned men may not have achieved what they did--a Black woman named Elizabeth Freeman. A woman most people have never heard of, but her example is one we must embrace as we enter the New Year. **Let 's Address This.**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bbcebb41-243b-4ea9-a225-b048f53d6b69?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
(L-R) Elizabeth Freeman, Daniel Frederick Bakeman, Alberty Henry Woolson, and President Jimmy Carter.
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..-l17GGQRxs4zbckfIe-jpK5IhYM0NeRRDd6WlR8nTGM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=153866898)
## The Past
Elizabeth Freeman was born in 1744 as an enslaved person and died on December 28, 1829 as a free woman. Unable to read, nor write, she solicited the help of an abolitionist lawyer named Theodore Sedgwick to fight for her freedom. In 1781 Freeman sued for her freedom. An all white jury convened by the Massachusetts Supreme Court agreed, and Freeman became the first person in U.S. history to win her freedom through the legal process. About her life Freeman would later declare:
> Any time, any time while I was a slave, if one minute's freedom had been offered to me, and I had been told I must die at the end of that minute, I would have taken it--just to stand one minute on God's _airth_ [sic] a free woman--I would.
As the struggles before us in 2025 seem insurmountable, I reflect on the struggles these four icons fought to achieve what they did. Demanding and winning freedom against insurmountable odds, establishing a nation against tyranny, uniting a nation ripped apart by slavery and white supremacy, and demanding universal human rights against overwhelming opposition. And while none of them were perfect (except probably Freeman), they each played a critical role in forming a more perfect Union.
So as I reflect on this history, I think about the fact that as a nation we have been here before, and yet that never stopped those committed to justice from relentlessly demanding and achieving it. Thus, I believe we can overcome the struggles that await us in 2025--provided our North Star is justice and that we remain united in our efforts. It will not be easy, and the struggles before us demonstrate how daunting a task it is.
## The Present
Today we see what seems to be an endless list of struggles of working families--families struggling to put food on table, immigrants and refugees struggling for basic recognition, working people struggling for healthcare access, Black, Indigenous, and people of color struggling against systemic racism, we witnessed an 18% increase in homelessness, and the list goes on and on.
Globally, we see Palestinians suffer genocide, Ukrainians fight off invasion, Congolese battle human trafficking, Afghanistan suffer violent patriarchy, Sudanese fight societal collapse, and Syrians struggle to build a new society, and again the list again goes on.
As each of these struggles and sufferings spill into 2025, we must remain informed, engaged, and ready to act. As James Baldwin reminds us, despair is not an option. Hopelessness is not an option. But taking action, is.
## The Future
Let me start with four action items each of us can accomplish in January:
1. Get involved with your local city council, school board, and/or county board. Attend their meetings, speak at these meetings, and understand the local issues. And where you see injustice, call it out and make your voice heard.
2. Get behind candidates committed to justice, that reject corporate and SuperPAC money, and who have a track record of condemning horrors like genocide. Donate to their campaigns, volunteer for their election and re-election, and share their content on social media.
3. Run for office. If you cannot find the right candidate who is committed to justice, become the candidate committed to justice and lead by example. Young people, women, underrepresented communities -- all of your voices are needed. Run.
4. Stay actively informed on the critical issues that impact us locally, nationally, and internationally. And on that, I hope you also invest in me to provide that insight.

_Let 's Address This_ is my struggle to elevate marginalized voices, bring attention and insight to neglected issues, and demand accountability of those in power. Normally writers offer some discount or 'new year sale' to subscribe. While I don't oppose that approach, I take a different path. I simply ensure my content is freely accessible to all readers, period. Whether you subscribe for free, or for $6/mo, $50/year, or as a Founding Member at $500--my advocacy is accessible to all because I don't believe in paywalls for access.
So then why subscribe at all?
Well, subscribe because being informed matters, and the better informed we are on the injustices we face, the better the actions we can take to counter those injustices. Elizabeth Freeman became informed that Article 1 of the newly ratified Massachusetts State Constitution declared, "_All men are born free and equal_ ," and used that as the foundation of her lawsuit--and in doing so both won her freedom and made history.
Subscribe because billionaires have bought media outlets to turn a clickbait profit, rather than to ensure Americans are informed on the things that matter. I've written about this [dangerous capitulation of corporate bought media](https://substack.com/redirect/62bcba44-8dc8-4801-bf20-cd8d003060f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) numerous times, and how it is simply unsustainable if we hope to uphold our democracy and human rights.
But finally, subscribe simply because you believe in me, in my struggle for human rights advocacy, and in the idea that we should invest in the people on the front lines of human rights issues working to uplift our society. I'm not funded by billionaires, and I'm not a billion dollar corporation. I'm a human rights lawyer doing everything in my power to uphold justice and build a better future for my children and yours.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..-l17GGQRxs4zbckfIe-jpK5IhYM0NeRRDd6WlR8nTGM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=153866898)
## In Closing...
We face major struggles in 2025. Different struggles, but not entirely dissimilar to the struggles battled by President Jimmy Carter, Civil War hero Albert Henry Woolson, Revolutionary War hero Daniel Frederick Bakeman, and human rights legend Elizabeth Freeman. And like them, we too shall advance and overcome, even if it is one small but consequential step at a time.
So, in 2025, may we successfully uphold justice in all we do, may we always protect the rights of the marginalized, and may we stand united with sincere good will towards our neighbors of every faith (or no faith), color, and nationality.
Indeed, may we finally form that more perfect Union--together.
I am grateful for your continued love, prayers, and _trust_ as we elevate our struggle for justice in 2025 and beyond. Thank you, and let's act to make it a Happy New Year.
Sincerely,
_Qasim Rashid, Esq._
_Founder, Let 's Address This_
_January 1, 2025_

But there's a fourth person we must acknowledge, without whom perhaps the aforementioned men may not have achieved what they did--a Black woman named Elizabeth Freeman. A woman most people have never heard of, but her example is one we must embrace as we enter the New Year. Let 's Address This. | | ---|---|--- (L-R) Elizabeth Freeman, Daniel Frederick Bakeman, Alberty Henry Woolson, and President Jimmy Carter. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. The Past Elizabeth Freeman was born in 1744 as an enslaved person and died on December 28, 1829 as a free woman. Unable to read, nor write, she solicited the help of an abolitionist lawyer named Theodore Sedgwick to fight for her freedom. In 1781 Freeman sued for her freedom. An all white jury convened by the Massachusetts Supreme Court agreed, and Freeman became the first person in U.S. history to win her freedom through the legal process. About her life Freeman would later declare: Any time, any time while I was a slave, if one minute's freedom had been offered to me, and I had been told I must die at the end of that minute, I would have taken it--just to stand one minute on God's airth [sic] a free woman--I would. As the struggles before us in 2025 seem insurmountable, I reflect on the struggles these four icons fought to achieve what they did. Demanding and winning freedom against insurmountable odds, establishing a nation against tyranny, uniting a nation ripped apart by slavery and white supremacy, and demanding universal human rights against overwhelming opposition. And while none of them were perfect (except probably Freeman), they each played a critical role in forming a more perfect Union. So as I reflect on this history, I think about the fact that as a nation we have been here before, and yet that never stopped those committed to justice from relentlessly demanding and achieving it. Thus, I believe we can overcome the struggles that await us in 2025--provided our North Star is justice and that we remain united in our efforts. It will not be easy, and the struggles before us demonstrate how daunting a task it is. The Present Today we see what seems to be an endless list of struggles of working families--families struggling to put food on table, immigrants and refugees struggling for basic recognition, working people struggling for healthcare access, Black, Indigenous, and people of color struggling against systemic racism, we witnessed an 18% increase in homelessness, and the list goes on and on. Globally, we see Palestinians suffer genocide, Ukrainians fight off invasion, Congolese battle human trafficking, Afghanistan suffer violent patriarchy, Sudanese fight societal collapse, and Syrians struggle to build a new society, and again the list again goes on. As each of these struggles and sufferings spill into 2025, we must remain informed, engaged, and ready to act. As James Baldwin reminds us, despair is not an option. Hopelessness is not an option. But taking action, is. The Future Let me start with four action items each of us can accomplish in January: 1. Get involved with your local city council, school board, and/or county board. Attend their meetings, speak at these meetings, and understand the local issues. And where you see injustice, call it out and make your voice heard. 2. Get behind candidates committed to justice, that reject corporate and SuperPAC money, and who have a track record of condemning horrors like genocide. Donate to their campaigns, volunteer for their election and re-election, and share their content on social media. 3. Run for office. If you cannot find the right candidate who is committed to justice, become the candidate committed to justice and lead by example. Young people, women, underrepresented communities -- all of your voices are needed. Run. 4. Stay actively informed on the critical issues that impact us locally, nationally, and internationally. And on that, I hope you also invest in me to provide that insight. Let 's Address This is my struggle to elevate marginalized voices, bring attention and insight to neglected issues, and demand accountability of those in power. Normally writers offer some discount or 'new year sale' to subscribe. While I don't oppose that approach, I take a different path. I simply ensure my content is freely accessible to all readers, period. Whether you subscribe for free, or for $6/mo, $50/year, or as a Founding Member at $500--my advocacy is accessible to all because I don't believe in paywalls for access. So then why subscribe at all? Well, subscribe because being informed matters, and the better informed we are on the injustices we face, the better the actions we can take to counter those injustices. Elizabeth Freeman became informed that Article 1 of the newly ratified Massachusetts State Constitution declared, "All men are born free and equal ," and used that as the foundation of her lawsuit--and in doing so both won her freedom and made history. Subscribe because billionaires have bought media outlets to turn a clickbait profit, rather than to ensure Americans are informed on the things that matter. I've written about this numerous times, and how it is simply unsustainable if we hope to uphold our democracy and human rights. But finally, subscribe simply because you believe in me, in my struggle for human rights advocacy, and in the idea that we should invest in the people on the front lines of human rights issues working to uplift our society. I'm not funded by billionaires, and I'm not a billion dollar corporation. I'm a human rights lawyer doing everything in my power to uphold justice and build a better future for my children and yours. In Closing... We face major struggles in 2025. Different struggles, but not entirely dissimilar to the struggles battled by President Jimmy Carter, Civil War hero Albert Henry Woolson, Revolutionary War hero Daniel Frederick Bakeman, and human rights legend Elizabeth Freeman. And like them, we too shall advance and overcome, even if it is one small but consequential step at a time. So, in 2025, may we successfully uphold justice in all we do, may we always protect the rights of the marginalized, and may we stand united with sincere good will towards our neighbors of every faith (or no faith), color, and nationality. Indeed, may we finally form that more perfect Union--together. I am grateful for your continued love, prayers, and trust as we elevate our struggle for justice in 2025 and beyond. Thank you, and let's act to make it a Happy New Year. Sincerely, Qasim Rashid, Esq. Founder, Let 's Address This January 1, 2025 Join the movement by becoming a free or paid subscriber. Quick Ask! I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.Become a paid subscriber and join the movement. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

name: Lara Trump
subject: Make no mistake, this is about all of America’s children.
body:
The Left wants kids lazy and unmotivated so they can control them.| | | You may know me as President Trump's daughter-in-law but this morning I'm writing you as a mother with one mission on my mind: To protect our children from the poison of woke ideology.
---
| |
---
| | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6152f1aa5c514b47863a8abe1be78998&_e=2YKJ9dprRAsl8pp0VEubRntNX_Cut25X_0OQcJK-hYryBXGHYtb_9AFKgvHLyFscm3aot987bZVpdd9XbztJc4Qj8JT2ThFWwWfQYtrTJdWYNtG55qmJVEgauU75M_QjLVHPRmPSS-oXI6tucvwHx0EO4mR8kVc4FVjzZeFEOYOY9qpttxB5hXXMDSS34g2UerRG8Z1rEra3b8iwmejd1ZvQUHo4FlkVFr9urat5YWHIG5zoo96AtLpgWS5YuUNg6Kq_NDTs_9G5XSaZc63i2ArA_FjySMAriSjVGVsc0o9S17FNB5mnFDQfbeW6sYE7Cc0iyg64mz_HjjdMvkyyj7F9hKh1TThWppDvQJ3aiHZISe8UUXrFI8DkDNxZA7RzE9Q706vp39LdstCaHu4GPQ%3D%3D)
---
Dear Fellow American, You may know me as President Trump's daughter-in-law but this morning I'm writing you as a mother with one mission on my mind: To protect our children from the poison of woke ideology.And to accomplish that, **[I need you to join the fight.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6152f1aa5c514b47863a8abe1be78998&_e=2YKJ9dprRAsl8pp0VEubRntNX_Cut25X_0OQcJK-hYryBXGHYtb_9AFKgvHLyFscm3aot987bZVpdd9XbztJc4Qj8JT2ThFWwWfQYtrTJdWYNtG55qmJVEgauU75M_QjLVHPRmPSS-oXI6tucvwHx0EO4mR8kVc4FVjzZeFEOYOY9qpttxB5hXXMDSS34g2UerRG8Z1rEra3b8iwmejd1ZvQUHo4FlkVFr9urat5YWHIG5zoo96AtLpgWS5YuUNgwRqmHCkckfJV_WMUyp7EdXRvh6WdfW2NwANMqfaeerm69-1RKxCtBGdvd8Cm23OeepvTex6H_QnzE9q24jFRCqrNB1RBA5PE1ZQ4WzWZ5v3vCy7xZrDq3mSGV-kR6MfLFR0dooa9lCG8du0LvRuGuA%3D%3D)** Make no mistake, this is about all of America's children. And how the Left is reaching right into our homes -- yours, mine, everyone's -- and luring our little ones into their anti-God, anti-America army.Before I go any further, if you're like many of the folks I've met across the country who've already raised their children, right now you may be thinking:"This is a parenting issue. Parents should just say, 'No!'" I understand. And I agree.But here's the reality.So many parents work hard every day to instill good, American values in our children.But the Left has its tentacles everywhere. I'm sure you know already how schools -- private and taxpayer funded -- are brimming with radicals trying to indoctrinate our children's minds and hearts.And you know that TV, movies, and music have all been hijacked by the Left.In nearly every space, the leftist messages creep in. Whether it's books the kids bring home from school, TV shows that claim to be "harmless," or even every other commercial pushing the leftist agenda......it's nearly impossible to keep the indoctrination out.That's why, together, you and I must do everything in our power to counter the Left's lies with THE TRUTH. **[**And that's why your support to Brave Books today is so critical.**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6152f1aa5c514b47863a8abe1be78998&_e=2YKJ9dprRAsl8pp0VEubRntNX_Cut25X_0OQcJK-hYryBXGHYtb_9AFKgvHLyFscm3aot987bZVpdd9XbztJc4Qj8JT2ThFWwWfQYtrTJdWYNtG55qmJVEgauU75M_QjLVHPRmPSS-oXI6tucvwHx0EO4mR8kVc4FVjzZeFEOYOY9qpttxB5hXXMDSS34g2UerRG8Z1rEra3b8iwmejd1ZvQUHo4FlkVFr9urat5YWHIG5zoo96AtLpgWS5YuUNgY5P11GHYHlqyXFJR8a3C-DlvI6chWIMUytH-UBhnYwudzNdo4noVeTGCOb0VmQcQXNsURbwVh388ZMsOGbmYj7uola4pE5hJl3HF3HTYkCp_rtfqhMedy5CD_dhwYZtp-svat92QfhWejuaDBNCGqw%3D%3D)** I'm going to tell you more about their critical mission in a minute but first I have to tell you about the most alarming thing parents are facing right now. Despite all parent's efforts, if their child takes the bait and latches onto a leftist lie......if parents show any opposition, the Left brings down all its might against them.Right now, Illinois is debating a bill that changes the definition of child abuse to include parents who refuse to let their minor children change genders.That is what we're up against.And so, all of us who love God and love our country must work together right now to instill rock-solid, armor of TRUTH into our children so they never fall for the Left's insane lies. **[**And the #1 thing you can do today is help by giving your best donation to Brave Books right now.**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=6152f1aa5c514b47863a8abe1be78998&_e=2YKJ9dprRAsl8pp0VEubRntNX_Cut25X_0OQcJK-hYryBXGHYtb_9AFKgvHLyFscm3aot987bZVpdd9XbztJc4Qj8JT2ThFWwWfQYtrTJdWYNtG55qmJVEgauU75M_QjLVHPRmPSS-oXI6tucvwHx0EO4mR8kVc4FVjzZeFEOYOY9qpttxB5hXXMDSS34g2UerRG8Z1rEra3b8iwmejd1ZvQUHo4FlkVFr9urat5YWHIG5zoo96AtLpgWS5YuUNgfWFDkM_q5ZPk-_nVbl47-JCgk9IbFJZeCqyrHHs9psRM-iYYTGCnyuOBF6hBy6swLZUy-e71GIlgLbTVSw8oml9bARe1ZWZK3wbIvpZbm3Cowrj1R4hcXAd27jt_6rpLRNVrpmVEYxADlO-pb7QY6g%3D%3D)** If you've heard of **Brave Books** , you know they're a mission-based company that arms pro-God, pro-American parents and grandparents with quality children’s books that captivate young minds and hearts with lessons in conservative values.Maybe you’ve seen them featured on _Fox News_ for countering the Left's horrible poison. **Brave Books** fought back by enlisting the help of actor, father, soon-to-be grandad, and Christian **Kirk Cameron** (_Growing Pains, Left Behind, Fireproof_) to host family friendly "See You at the Library" events across the country."See you at the Library" events proudly start with the Pledge of Allegiance followed by story time featuring great **Brave Books** with conservative messages such as:
---
* _The Test of Lionhood_ by **Kevin Sorbo --** Teaches children that masculinity and bravery are good.
* _The Night the Snow Monster Attacked_ by **General Michael Flynn --** Shows children what it takes to be a good leader.
* _Happy No Snake Day_ by**Riley Gaines --** Encourages children to stand up for the truth.
* _Because You're My Family_ by **Missy Robertson --** Teaches children the importance of family and unconditional Christian love.

---
| | Kirk Cameron himself has authored several **Brave Books**. But my favorite is _Pride Comes Before the Fall_ which teaches the beauty of humility in the eyes of God. The folks at **Brave Books** aren’t afraid to confront the "woke" Left head on!That's why when they asked me to partner with them on a book of my own, I jumped at the chance.My **Brave Book** , inspired in part by my father-in-law, is entitled _The Never-Give-Up Pup_ and it teaches the importance of hard work and perseverance. The Left wants kids lazy and unmotivated so they can control them.In _The Never-Give-Up Pup_ , kids learn how good it feels to put in a hard day’s work and see the results on the other side.Strong kids, brave kids, Godly kids are who Eric and I are trying to raise. And how the Left is reaching right into our homes -- yours, mine, everyone's -- and luring our little ones into their anti-God, anti-America army.Before I go any further, if you're like many of the folks I've met across the country who've already raised their children, right now you may be thinking:"This is a parenting issue. Parents should just say, 'No!'" I understand. And I agree.But here's the reality.So many parents work hard every day to instill good, American values in our children.But the Left has its tentacles everywhere. I'm sure you know already how schools -- private and taxpayer funded -- are brimming with radicals trying to indoctrinate our children's minds and hearts.And you know that TV, movies, and music have all been hijacked by the Left.In nearly every space, the leftist messages creep in. Whether it's books the kids bring home from school, TV shows that claim to be "harmless," or even every other commercial pushing the leftist agenda......it's nearly impossible to keep the indoctrination out.That's why, together, you and I must do everything in our power to counter the Left's lies with THE TRUTH. I'm going to tell you more about their critical mission in a minute but first I have to tell you about the most alarming thing parents are facing right now. Despite all parent's efforts, if their child takes the bait and latches onto a leftist lie......if parents show any opposition, the Left brings down all its might against them.Right now, Illinois is debating a bill that changes the definition of child abuse to include parents who refuse to let their minor children change genders.That is what we're up against.And so, all of us who love God and love our country must work together right now to instill rock-solid, armor of TRUTH into our children so they never fall for the Left's insane lies. The Night the Snow Monster Attacked by General Michael Flynn -- Shows children what it takes to be a good leader. Happy No Snake Day byRiley Gaines -- Encourages children to stand up for the truth. | | As we celebrate the coming of a new year, I want to take a moment to express my sincere gratitude for your continued support. 2024 was a year of resounding victories, and I am proud of what we’ve accomplished together.

---

| | In the year ahead, I will continue to stand up for the values that matter most to you and ensure that our voices are heard in Washington. Together, we can build a brighter future for all residents of the 17th District and New York State.
I wish you and your loved ones much health, happiness, and success in 2025.
Happy New Year!
Mike Lawler

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/paulany21/p/a-bold-courageous-plan-for-the-middle?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9fc84d9f-768b-456b-9c6a-16a1e6fcf3cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The issues are nested like Russian Dolls.
Hm. Fancy that -- Russian.
But you know what I mean - the dolls that open to reveal another doll, and then another doll.
By "issues" I mean the things that are holding back America. More specifically, I mean the issues that are holding back America's middle class. Open up one issue, and it leads to another, and another, and still others.
### What happened to prompt me to write this column?
In a bit of good news, it seems that the nested issues of the middle class might expose fissures in MAGA world.
Weeks before Trump is sworn in to office, his Donor-In-Chief, Elon Musk, is catching flak from MAGA supporters for his [remarks](https://substack.com/redirect/11cc0206-e1cc-4aa6-9bf6-1c241d08880a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) regarding a "dire shortage of extremely talented and motivated engineers in America".
Musk called for an increase in the number of H-1B visas for highly-skilled workers to be employed in Silicon Valley. (The H-1B Visa is the designation for the work visa necessary if a company wishes to hire someone who is not a U.S. citizen.)
The MAGAs, arguing on Musk's own platform, X, insisted that of the 330 million people in America, surely Musk could employ Americans for those tech jobs.
A businessman, also on X, said that the semiconductor industry alone needs 160,000 jobs by 2032. Musk quipped back, saying that the need is double that, and those workers were needed "yesterday."
Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-director of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), jumped into the fray. Dan Rather, writing on Substack reported this:
> Ramaswamy took to X to make his point. "A culture that celebrates the prom queen over the math Olympiad champ, or the jock over the Valedictorian, will not produce the best engineers," he wrote. "More math tutoring, fewer sleepovers. More weekend science competitions, fewer Saturday morning cartoons. More books, less TV. More creating, less 'chillin.' More extracurriculars, less 'hanging out at the mall.'"
> Whoa. That didn't go over well with the old-school MAGA types who are now calling Ramaswamy and Musk many names, including the deepest cut of all, "communist." Former Trump adviser Steve Bannon said, "The H-1B visa program is a total and complete scam from its top to the bottom." Trump acolyte Laura Loomer says Musk "bought his way into MAGA" and is a "stage five clinger" who will soon find himself divorced from Trump.
For a split second, I felt sorry for Musk (don't worry, that soon passed). I have had more than a few tense arguments with MAGA supporters over immigration issues. There's no winning such arguments. And Ramaswamy's criticisms of school culture? His statements cut to the heart of any small town or village in upstate New York. Clearly he hasn't traveled much in the US, or he would know -- ya just don't say stuff like that.
If a Democrat had said anything like what Ramaswamy said, the small blue dots we see on the electoral map would be swept into the red sea forever.
Musk held in there, saying, "OF COURSE my companies and I would prefer to hire Americans and we DO, as that is MUCH easier than going through the incredibly painful and slow work visa process."
"The number of people who are super talented engineers AND super motivated in the USA is far too low."
Musk [came to the US in 1992](https://substack.com/redirect/f35f6341-dee5-453b-be7a-12742c4336dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a South African-born Canadian citizen (his mother has Canadian citizenship) to study at University of Pennsylvania.
It is possible that Musk is only now coming face-to-face with the shortfalls in our education system. Our current trajectory sets us on a permanent and escalating shortage of skilled tech workers.
It is also possible that Musk sparked an internal squabble that may divide MAGA.
### Math education
Musk's lack of highly-skilled tech workers is due to a lack of workforce-ready, motivated graduates who have higher math skills. Evidently, we don't have the math education in the U.S. to support a robust workforce of engineers. [A Pew Research study](https://substack.com/redirect/b9edcd12-53a7-48cc-8079-76db38289913?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that the U.S. ranks 28th out of 37 countries in math. That's not enough to create a solid workforce of above-average math geeks to fill Musk's factories and development labs.
A simple search on Google (another employer of vast numbers of H-1B Visa holders) reveals that our [very approach to math education is out of step](https://substack.com/redirect/6405745f-8881-4fb2-85ac-8b05b7bbc161?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with other nations.
We even put the validity of basic math education into doubt, such as the question of [whether a student should be required to memorize multiplication tables](https://substack.com/redirect/30427d08-c8e2-4d54-82d8-38bcac1cdd08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### Who ya gonna' hire, New York?
Musk's highlight of the need for H-1B Visa holders to staff semiconductor manufacturing jobs should send shudders across upstate New York, which is slated to open microchip factories in multiple sites in the next 10 years thanks to the CHIPS Act. It begs the question: Who ya' gonna hire?
### How did we get here?
This did not happen over night. It's been decades in the making.
Jimmy Carter is on my mind lately. Adjusted for inflation, the $2.30 minimum wage in Jimmy Carter's 1976 economy equals $12.76 in today's dollars. Wow. That sounds like a lot, doesn't it?
I have deep admiration for Jimmy Carter, but it wasn't an easy time. Remember the gas lines? Remember high interest rates and inflation? I'm a true-blue Jimmy Carter fan, but the 1970s are the decade in which it all began to unravel for the American middle class. And the Reagan Revolution that followed, coupled with deregulation and anti-union sentiment, pushed the middle class down the economic slide from which we have never recovered.
### Let's Crunch Some Numbers
The federal minimum wage as of January 1, 2025 is $7.25. In upstate New York, thanks to our state government, the minimum wage is $15.50 as of January 1, 2025. Keep in mind, however, that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) has almost sextupled since then, measured at 56 in January 1976 and at 302.21 in January 2024. In other words, the cost of basic consumer goods has multiplied by a factor of almost 6 since Jimmy Carter's time. If we wanted the same strength of the minimum wage that people experienced during the Carter administration, plus adjustments for the consumer price index, we should take the $12.76 and multiply it by 6, for a minimum wage of $76.56.
"Outrageous", you say. $76.56 an hour for minimum wage? Well gosh, the base price for one of Elon Musk's Teslas is slightly more than $40,000. And that's a base price model. (Does it even include the charging cable?) Chances are high that the buyer is going to set up $720+ payments on a 7-year plan, with interest. You see how rapidly that huge hourly wage gets chiseled away.
You can be sure, the highly-skilled tech employees who are coming over to work in our US tech industry are not earning minimum wage, especially when you consider stock grants and bonuses. Google employees can earn base pay of up to $718,000, plus stock options and bonuses. No wonder they're packing their bags and heading for the nearest airport.
### How does the low wage earned by a parent impact the kids' math scores?
With such disparity in wages, and under-earning by 6 times compared to 1976, parents, who are often working two jobs each for a total of 4 jobs per household, are unable or simply too exhausted to quiz their children at the dinner table on their multiplication tables. If they are able to shop with their kids in tow, they are too distracted to do everyday math with their children, such as having them calculate the weight of bananas and the resulting price. Parents working two jobs may miss one, or two, or more, parent-teacher conferences to discuss their child's progress in math.
In other words, one level of the nesting of the proverbial dolls I mentioned above comes about with low wages, resulting in over-worked and unavailable parents, which then results in low performance in our schools.
### Who's minding the kids while Mommy and Daddy work?
Talk to anyone with a school-age kid and you know that childcare is a major juggling act. If not provided by a family member, it is also a huge expense, one that rivals the housing payment.
We know that Pre-K can set a child on the right path to literacy and numeracy. So too, can the right after school program, Saturday or Sunday School, summer camp, or academic tutoring.
Increasingly, these are options reserved for upper-income parents. The middle class families can forget fancy preschools, math tutors, Saturday and after school programs to boost math scores. Forget SAT prep programs. Most parents are doing well to keep a reasonable cycle of hand-me-down clothes in the household.
Yet the lack of enrichment activities -- even those things like music lessons and chess clubs that have a derivative effect on development of math skills, forms a barrier to admission to top colleges and universities. And that, in turn has the potential to limit one's job opportunities.
### Buying a house on minimum wage
The Great American Dream and fastest way to accumulate wealth -- home ownership -- yields yet another nested issue. The minimum wage was originally conceived as a wage sufficient to support a family. That $76.56 I mentioned above, which equates to annualized pay of $159,245 per year, would make it possible for someone to buy a 3-bedroom home in upstate New York. The naysayers reading this will tell me that there is a house for sale down the county road from them for $89,000, so I'm just plain crazy to suggest such an impossibly-high minimum wage. Maybe. But how much rehab does that $89,000 need to make it livable for a family? How much does it cost to heat? How much does it cost to insure? Does it have enough bedrooms for a family of 4?
The inventory of turn-key houses in upstate New York also includes a lot of houses for sale in the millions. (Even some of those homes require rehab.) Let's set an average house price at $250,000. Get ready for the down payment, which you need to scrape and save from disposable cash after you've bought your very expensive car to get to-and-from work, plus food, gas, and rent while you're saving. And, by the way, even the $89,000 home is out of reach for the person earning the NY minimum wage of $15.50, since they would have to come up with a down payment and closing costs. Chances are high that their credit score is encumbered by car payments, credit card debt, and medical debt that is in collection.
### Forget buying a house. What about rent?
With home ownership out of reach, let's talk about the cost of rent. The average rent for anything other than a studio apartment will run $1200, plus utilities. Utilities are going to run around $150, plus internet/cable/phone for another $120. (These are rough figures; not exact.) Talk to someone who is living in a rental about an upgrade and they immediately tell you they can't afford to come up with the deposit.
With a minimum wage of $15.50 in upstate New York, the weekly take home pay after taxes is $467. You see the struggle. Even if you are not one of Musk's math geeks, you can see that we're out of money long before we are out of days in the month.
### Don't forget about teacher salaries
Of course, wage suppression carries over to the education workforce. Average teacher salaries also have not kept pace with the consumer price index, despite advocacy from teachers' unions. So we have an underpaid teacher, working to educate kids from underpaid households, whose underpaid parents are unable to invest the out-of-school time to make sure that the times tables are learned.
Sound stressful? Most people would not last until lunch in an American public school, for any amount of money. America's teachers should be honored and respected for what is clearly an underpaid labor of love. Musk's workforce challenges will be further exacerbated if we can't raise teacher salaries. I won't even get into the other social issues in schools, such as whether or not to arm teachers to defend against active shooters, and monitoring bathrooms and girls' sports.
### Another nested issue: Healthcare
Healthcare. The cost of healthcare, and the lack of providers in a lot of areas (also a result of poor performance in math) results in the underpaid parent having to take time from work (usually without pay) to spend a large part of the day to take the child to the doctor. In upstate New York, things like telehealth could make things better, but providers place bizarre requirements like forcing people to drive 45 minutes to an office in order to speak with a doctor over video chat. To see a doctor in person, many people in upstate New York are driving 2 to 3 hours. Each way.
And then, once the doctor's visit is clncluded, there is the sky-high cost of prescription drugs.
The average family of four, if paying for an unsubsidized plan, needs to budget $1500-$2000 per month for health insurance. That's just the premium. If the deductible hasn't been met, there's another expense. Quick -- go online and apply for that extra job. You're not gonna be able to afford health insurance without it.
Health insurance in this country. Fortunately, in New York, we have a robust Medicaid system. Depending on one's income, it is possible to have zero premium. It's the people in the above-minimum-wage range who really suffer. Even on a sliding scale, it's really, really tough for families to make it work.
### Depression + Addiction = Lack of Motivated Workforce
And then there is the resulting depression and addiction that accompanies a life filled with financial stress, expensive or inaccessible healthcare, and the challenges of raising children in a school system with underpaid teachers.
It's depressing. It makes us vulnerable to any substance that will make us feel better, if even for a moment. Our patchwork of federally-subsidized, state-supported addiction recovery centers is just that - a patchwork.
No wonder we have a workforce characterized by lack of motivation.
In rural areas, a person who goes to a treatment facility is usually several hours' drive away for residential treatment, with aftercare and ongoing therapy unavailable once they reintegrate back into their home communities. The cycle of addiction continues, with any available family or support system worn down sometime after the second stint in rehab.
### Thanks for kick-starting the conversation, Elon
Musk's frustration about an under-skilled, under-educated, under-motivated workforce is no surprise. It's been in the making since long before Musk came to the U.S. on a student Visa in the 1990s, to take a seat that otherwise might have gone to an American student, except for the poor performance on the SAT.
### A Bold, Courageous Plan for the Middle Class
Musk's Twitter war last week on his need to employ vast numbers of immigrants on H-1B Visas may give fodder to Democrats delivering the message that we can't just fix immigration, or wages, or housing, or healthcare, or mental health and addiction. No. We need a comprehensive plan that addresses all of the nested issues that have led to the suppression of the middle class.
I propose a bold, courageous plan that provides for a clear path to immigration (not just for H-1B work visas.
We must do more. America's middle class needs a comprehensive plan that wraps around wages, housing, education, childcare, and healthcare.
I propose a comprehensive plan that raises wages to a living wage, replenishes our inventory of housing in each county in the US based on the most recent federal census, federally-subsidized daycare and school enrichment programs for after school care, and Medicare for All that includes mental health care.
How will we pay for it? Tax the top earners. Tax the corporations. And actually collect the taxes, which means we need to keep that funding for the IRS that was approved a few years ago. We can't let corporations and top earners take deductions, depreciation, and tax credits to dodge the tax liabilities.
In 1976, when the federal minimum wage was $2.30, the maximum corporate tax rate was 48%.
Donald Trump, in his first administration, lowered the maximum corporate tax rate to 21%. He has said he wants to [lower it again to 15%](https://substack.com/redirect/503d987a-3e68-4dfe-957e-1a6d0677e4a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The only way to balance the federal government will be to cut SNAP, Social Security, and Medicare.
In their gaffe to connect the need for immigrant visas to the lack of math education in our country, the DOGE Duo handed us a very strong foundation for a platform of reform.
Thanks for kick-starting the conversation, Elon.
_If we haven 't met yet, I am Paula Collins, the Democrat who ran in New York's 21st Congressional District. I hope to run in the Special Election in 2025. Your comments are invaluable to me. Please let me hear from you. Remember that you can always visit my website at www.paulacollinsforcongress.com or by sending me a text at 315-230-3920. _

Hm. Fancy that -- Russian. But you know what I mean - the dolls that open to reveal another doll, and then another doll. By "issues" I mean the things that are holding back America. More specifically, I mean the issues that are holding back America's middle class. Open up one issue, and it leads to another, and another, and still others. What happened to prompt me to write this column? In a bit of good news, it seems that the nested issues of the middle class might expose fissures in MAGA world. Weeks before Trump is sworn in to office, his Donor-In-Chief, Elon Musk, is catching flak from MAGA supporters for his regarding a "dire shortage of extremely talented and motivated engineers in America". Musk called for an increase in the number of H-1B visas for highly-skilled workers to be employed in Silicon Valley. (The H-1B Visa is the designation for the work visa necessary if a company wishes to hire someone who is not a U.S. citizen.) The MAGAs, arguing on Musk's own platform, X, insisted that of the 330 million people in America, surely Musk could employ Americans for those tech jobs. A businessman, also on X, said that the semiconductor industry alone needs 160,000 jobs by 2032. Musk quipped back, saying that the need is double that, and those workers were needed "yesterday." Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-director of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), jumped into the fray. Dan Rather, writing on Substack reported this: Ramaswamy took to X to make his point. "A culture that celebrates the prom queen over the math Olympiad champ, or the jock over the Valedictorian, will not produce the best engineers," he wrote. "More math tutoring, fewer sleepovers. More weekend science competitions, fewer Saturday morning cartoons. More books, less TV. More creating, less 'chillin.' More extracurriculars, less 'hanging out at the mall.'" Whoa. That didn't go over well with the old-school MAGA types who are now calling Ramaswamy and Musk many names, including the deepest cut of all, "communist." Former Trump adviser Steve Bannon said, "The H-1B visa program is a total and complete scam from its top to the bottom." Trump acolyte Laura Loomer says Musk "bought his way into MAGA" and is a "stage five clinger" who will soon find himself divorced from Trump. For a split second, I felt sorry for Musk (don't worry, that soon passed). I have had more than a few tense arguments with MAGA supporters over immigration issues. There's no winning such arguments. And Ramaswamy's criticisms of school culture? His statements cut to the heart of any small town or village in upstate New York. Clearly he hasn't traveled much in the US, or he would know -- ya just don't say stuff like that. If a Democrat had said anything like what Ramaswamy said, the small blue dots we see on the electoral map would be swept into the red sea forever. Musk held in there, saying, "OF COURSE my companies and I would prefer to hire Americans and we DO, as that is MUCH easier than going through the incredibly painful and slow work visa process." "The number of people who are super talented engineers AND super motivated in the USA is far too low." Musk as a South African-born Canadian citizen (his mother has Canadian citizenship) to study at University of Pennsylvania. It is possible that Musk is only now coming face-to-face with the shortfalls in our education system. Our current trajectory sets us on a permanent and escalating shortage of skilled tech workers. It is also possible that Musk sparked an internal squabble that may divide MAGA. Math education Musk's lack of highly-skilled tech workers is due to a lack of workforce-ready, motivated graduates who have higher math skills. Evidently, we don't have the math education in the U.S. to support a robust workforce of engineers. found that the U.S. ranks 28th out of 37 countries in math. That's not enough to create a solid workforce of above-average math geeks to fill Musk's factories and development labs. A simple search on Google (another employer of vast numbers of H-1B Visa holders) reveals that our with other nations. We even put the validity of basic math education into doubt, such as the question of . Who ya gonna' hire, New York? Musk's highlight of the need for H-1B Visa holders to staff semiconductor manufacturing jobs should send shudders across upstate New York, which is slated to open microchip factories in multiple sites in the next 10 years thanks to the CHIPS Act. It begs the question: Who ya' gonna hire? How did we get here? This did not happen over night. It's been decades in the making. Jimmy Carter is on my mind lately. Adjusted for inflation, the $2.30 minimum wage in Jimmy Carter's 1976 economy equals $12.76 in today's dollars. Wow. That sounds like a lot, doesn't it? I have deep admiration for Jimmy Carter, but it wasn't an easy time. Remember the gas lines? Remember high interest rates and inflation? I'm a true-blue Jimmy Carter fan, but the 1970s are the decade in which it all began to unravel for the American middle class. And the Reagan Revolution that followed, coupled with deregulation and anti-union sentiment, pushed the middle class down the economic slide from which we have never recovered. Let's Crunch Some Numbers The federal minimum wage as of January 1, 2025 is $7.25. In upstate New York, thanks to our state government, the minimum wage is $15.50 as of January 1, 2025. Keep in mind, however, that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) has almost sextupled since then, measured at 56 in January 1976 and at 302.21 in January 2024. In other words, the cost of basic consumer goods has multiplied by a factor of almost 6 since Jimmy Carter's time. If we wanted the same strength of the minimum wage that people experienced during the Carter administration, plus adjustments for the consumer price index, we should take the $12.76 and multiply it by 6, for a minimum wage of $76.56. "Outrageous", you say. $76.56 an hour for minimum wage? Well gosh, the base price for one of Elon Musk's Teslas is slightly more than $40,000. And that's a base price model. (Does it even include the charging cable?) Chances are high that the buyer is going to set up $720+ payments on a 7-year plan, with interest. You see how rapidly that huge hourly wage gets chiseled away. You can be sure, the highly-skilled tech employees who are coming over to work in our US tech industry are not earning minimum wage, especially when you consider stock grants and bonuses. Google employees can earn base pay of up to $718,000, plus stock options and bonuses. No wonder they're packing their bags and heading for the nearest airport. How does the low wage earned by a parent impact the kids' math scores? With such disparity in wages, and under-earning by 6 times compared to 1976, parents, who are often working two jobs each for a total of 4 jobs per household, are unable or simply too exhausted to quiz their children at the dinner table on their multiplication tables. If they are able to shop with their kids in tow, they are too distracted to do everyday math with their children, such as having them calculate the weight of bananas and the resulting price. Parents working two jobs may miss one, or two, or more, parent-teacher conferences to discuss their child's progress in math. In other words, one level of the nesting of the proverbial dolls I mentioned above comes about with low wages, resulting in over-worked and unavailable parents, which then results in low performance in our schools. Who's minding the kids while Mommy and Daddy work? Talk to anyone with a school-age kid and you know that childcare is a major juggling act. If not provided by a family member, it is also a huge expense, one that rivals the housing payment. We know that Pre-K can set a child on the right path to literacy and numeracy. So too, can the right after school program, Saturday or Sunday School, summer camp, or academic tutoring. Increasingly, these are options reserved for upper-income parents. The middle class families can forget fancy preschools, math tutors, Saturday and after school programs to boost math scores. Forget SAT prep programs. Most parents are doing well to keep a reasonable cycle of hand-me-down clothes in the household. Yet the lack of enrichment activities -- even those things like music lessons and chess clubs that have a derivative effect on development of math skills, forms a barrier to admission to top colleges and universities. And that, in turn has the potential to limit one's job opportunities. Buying a house on minimum wage The Great American Dream and fastest way to accumulate wealth -- home ownership -- yields yet another nested issue. The minimum wage was originally conceived as a wage sufficient to support a family. That $76.56 I mentioned above, which equates to annualized pay of $159,245 per year, would make it possible for someone to buy a 3-bedroom home in upstate New York. The naysayers reading this will tell me that there is a house for sale down the county road from them for $89,000, so I'm just plain crazy to suggest such an impossibly-high minimum wage. Maybe. But how much rehab does that $89,000 need to make it livable for a family? How much does it cost to heat? How much does it cost to insure? Does it have enough bedrooms for a family of 4? Chances are high that their credit score is encumbered by car payments, credit card debt, and medical debt that is in collection. Forget buying a house. What about rent? With home ownership out of reach, let's talk about the cost of rent. The average rent for anything other than a studio apartment will run $1200, plus utilities. Utilities are going to run around $150, plus internet/cable/phone for another $120. (These are rough figures; not exact.) Talk to someone who is living in a rental about an upgrade and they immediately tell you they can't afford to come up with the deposit. The cost of healthcare, and the lack of providers in a lot of areas (also a result of poor performance in math) results in the underpaid parent having to take time from work (usually without pay) to spend a large part of the day to take the child to the doctor. Patriot,

In New Jersey, Republicans **FOUGHT BACK** against the Radical Democrats who've turned the Garden State into a **_breeding ground for high taxes, government overreach, and policies that hurt working families._**

And we didn't just fight--we **WON** :

\- Flipping 7 counties from blue to _**RED**_.
\- Seeing 18 out of 21 counties swing **DOUBLE DIGITS** toward Trump.
\- CRUSHING the Democrats in voter registration--outpacing them **3 to 1**.

This is **UNPRECEDENTED**.

New Jersey is at a tipping point. **_We 're closer than ever to flipping this battleground state and turning it into a beacon of freedom and prosperity for all._**
**
But the fight is far from over.**

The Democrats are **DESPERATE**. Their machine is already working overtime to undo our progress and push their radical agenda even further. They'll stop at **NOTHING** to hold onto power.
**
That 's why I'm reaching out to you.**

Right now, **we 're $25,000 short of our End-of-Year goal.**

If we fall short, we'll enter 2025 at a disadvantage against the Radical Left's well-funded war chest.

**But here 's the good news:** _If every supporter reading this email chips in**just $10** , we'll shatter our goal and take on the Radical Machine head-on._

I want to wish you a very happy 2025.

name: Grover Norquist
subject: a personal favor
body:
We are at a pivotal moment in our nation's history.

*|MC_PREVIEW_TEXT|* |
---
| | [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/oCW8FRU8H474IbxZbssZEQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpWBY8P0RdaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9nb2xkd2F0ZXItaW5zdGl0dXRlL3BhLWFjdGlvbj91dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTAxMDEmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9YWZyVwNzcGNCCmdl-X91Z7XXfhFSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAAAAA)
Friend,

We are at a pivotal moment in our nation's history. The values that have made America a beacon of freedom and prosperity are under relentless attack.

As a steadfast advocate for limited government and individual liberty, I have had the honor of working alongside some of the nation's most dedicated and principled organizations.

**Today, I am reaching out to you on behalf of one such organization that has consistently championed the values we hold dear: the Goldwater Institute.**

The Goldwater Institute is on the front lines of this battle, scoring real wins for freedom in state courts, legislatures, and communities nationwide. But they cannot do it alone.

[I am personally helping the Goldwater Institute raise $50,000 this month. Your support is critical for us to continue our success in scoring real wins for freedom from coast to coast and empowering all Americans to live freer, happier lives. Can you join me by donating today? Click here to donate >>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/oCW8FRU8H474IbxZbssZEQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpWBY8P0RdaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9nb2xkd2F0ZXItaW5zdGl0dXRlL3BhLWFjdGlvbj91dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTAxMDEmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9YWZyVwNzcGNCCmdl-X91Z7XXfhFSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAAAAA)

The Goldwater Institute is at the forefront of the fight to defend America’s founding values, crafting and promoting policy solutions that empower individuals, reduce government overreach, and safeguard our constitutional rights.

From healthcare reform to educational choice, the Goldwater Institute's impact resonates across Arizona and the entire country.

As a board member, I have witnessed firsthand the incredible work the Goldwater Institute does. Their commitment to free-market principles and limited government is unwavering. **_The Goldwater Institute is not just talking about change; they are making it happen_.**

But to continue their critical work, they need the support of dedicated patriots like you. Your contribution to the Goldwater Institute will help:
1. **Defend Individual Liberties:** By challenging unconstitutional laws that infringe on our rights.
2. **Promote Economic Freedom:** Through policies that reduce the tax burden, eliminate unnecessary regulations, and foster a thriving business environment.
3. **Expand School Choice:** Ensuring that every child has access to the best possible education, regardless of their zip code or economic status.
4. **Protect Healthcare Freedom:** Advocating for patient-centered reforms that put control back into the hands of individuals and families.

[I urge you to join me in supporting the Goldwater Institute by making a generous donation today. Your investment in this vital work will help secure a future where freedom, opportunity, and prosperity flourish for all Americans.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/oCW8FRU8H474IbxZbssZEQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpWBY8P0RdaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS9nb2xkd2F0ZXItaW5zdGl0dXRlL3BhLWFjdGlvbj91dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTAxMDEmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9YWZyVwNzcGNCCmdl-X91Z7XXfhFSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAAAAA)

Peter,

I wanted to reach out personally and thank you for your incredible support in 2024.

As we enter the new year, I am so grateful for the friends, family, and supporters like you who have been by my side every step of the way. This year, many of us are feeling uncertain about what the future will bring. But I know if we work together, we can continue building a better future for all of us.

Thank you for standing with me in this fight. It’s supporters like you who are the most important members of our team.

Onward,

Seth

clean_body: Every special election marks a chance to protect or expand Democratic seats in statehouses nationwide – $7 helps our party make the most of every opportunity to build power. | | | | | ---|--- Next week, we’ll face our first challenges of 2025 – three special elections for seats in the Virginia House and Senate. | State | Chamber | District ---|---|--- Virginia | House | HD-26 Virginia | Senate | SD-10 Virginia | Senate | SD-32 Democrats hold the Virginia Senate by an ultra-thin margin. Holding our ground in these special elections is crucial to defending control of the chamber against a Republican power grab. President Trump has selected Vivek Ramaswmany and Elon Musk to lead the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).

This is an exciting development, and there's a lot of work to be done to cut the waste.

name: Kelly Ayotte
subject: Happy New Year!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
Wishing you a wonderful 2025 filled with family, friends, and freedom. | | | | |
---
| | | | | Happy New Year, Friend!
2024 was an amazing year of traveling across our beautiful state, meeting so many great people out on the campaign trail, and spending time with Joe, Kate, Jake, and Thunder. Now, I’m looking forward to hitting the ground running for New Hampshire in 2025!
As I take office next week, please know how grateful Joe and I are for your support. It is truly the honor of my life to serve our wonderful state as your next Governor. Thank you for your hard work and your kind words!
Wishing you a wonderful 2025 filled with family, friends, and freedom.
Best wishes,
Kelly
---
| Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
